---
title: "Thesis"
author: "Geoffrey Olweny"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: pdf_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```
## Read in the files

```{r}
#Set working directory
#setwd("AMR)
#list.files
```
## Load packages
```{r}
library(readr)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(viridis)  # For color palette
library(dplyr)
library(stringr)
library(cowplot)
```

## Create dataframe

```{r}
setwd("D:/mrsa/abricate/AMR/")
# List all summary.txt files in the directory
file_list <- list.files(pattern = "summary\\.txt$", full.names = TRUE)

# Initialize an empty list to store data frames
df_list <- list()

# Define the expected column names
common_cols <- c("FILE", "SEQUENCE", "START", "END", "STRAND", "GENE", 
                 "COVERAGE", "COVERAGE_MAP", "GAPS", "%COVERAGE", 
                 "%IDENTITY", "DATABASE", "ACCESSION", "PRODUCT", "RESISTANCE")

# Loop through each file and read it into a data frame
for (file in file_list) {
  # Check if the file is not empty
  if (file.info(file)$size > 0) {
    # Try to read the file and skip if there's an error
    try({
      df <- read.table(file, header = TRUE, sep = "\t", fill = TRUE, comment.char = "", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
      
      # Ensure that we have at least the expected number of columns (to avoid NAs)
      if (ncol(df) >= length(common_cols)) {
        # Subset the dataframe to keep only the expected columns, or add missing ones
        colnames(df) <- common_cols[1:ncol(df)]
        missing_cols <- setdiff(common_cols, colnames(df))
        
        # Add missing columns with NA values if necessary
        if (length(missing_cols) > 0) {
          df[missing_cols] <- NA
        }
        
        # Reorder columns to match the common structure
        df <- df[, common_cols]
        
        # Store each valid data frame in the list
        df_list[[file]] <- df
      } else {
        cat("Skipping file due to insufficient columns:", file, "\n")
      }
    }, silent = TRUE)
  } else {
    cat("Skipping empty file:", file, "\n")
  }
}

# Combine all valid data frames into one
final_df <- do.call(rbind, df_list)

# Optionally, you can write the combined dataframe to a new file
write.csv(final_df, "merged_summary.csv", row.names = TRUE)

# Print the combined data frame to check
print(final_df)


```


```{r}
# Extract genome IDs from the FILE column
final_df$GENOME_ID <- gsub(".*(ERR[0-9]+).*", "\\1", final_df$FILE)

# Define genome ID ranges for each country
uganda_ids <- paste0("ERR", 4692235:4692276)
kenya_ids <- c(
  paste0("ERR", 3150908:3151001), "ERR3218227",
  paste0("ERR", 1764887:1765052),
  paste0("ERR", 1791869:1791882),
  paste0("ERR", 2436451:2436455))
tanzania_ids <- c(
  paste0("ERR", 12916291:12916300),
  paste0("ERR", 12511686:12511850))

# Create a mapping dataframe
country_mapping <- data.frame(
  GENOME_ID = c(uganda_ids, kenya_ids, tanzania_ids),
  COUNTRY = c(
    rep("Uganda", length(uganda_ids)),
    rep("Kenya", length(kenya_ids)),
    rep("Tanzania", length(tanzania_ids))
  )
)

# Merge country information into final_df
final_df <- merge(final_df, country_mapping, by = "GENOME_ID", all.x = TRUE)

# View the updated dataframe
head(final_df)
```


```{r}
setwd("D:/mrsa/abricate/AMR/")

library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(viridis)  # For color palette

# Count occurrences of each resistance type for each gene
resistance_counts <- final_df %>%
  group_by(GENE, RESISTANCE, PRODUCT, ACCESSION, COUNTRY) %>%
  summarise(count = n(), .groups = 'drop')

# Filter out empty and NA resistance types
resistance_counts <- resistance_counts %>%
  filter(RESISTANCE != "" & !is.na(RESISTANCE))

# Summing the counts for each GENE across different RESISTANCE types
gene_totals <- resistance_counts %>%
  group_by(GENE) %>%
  summarise(total_count = sum(count), .groups = 'drop') %>%
  arrange(desc(total_count))  # Sort by total counts

# Get the top 20 genes with the highest overall counts
top_gene_totals <- gene_totals %>%
  slice_head(n = 20)  # Select the top 20 genes

# Plotting the top 20 genes with total counts
q <- ggplot(top_gene_totals, aes(x = reorder(GENE, total_count), y = total_count)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "skyblue") +
  
  # Add labels for total counts
  geom_text(aes(label = total_count), 
            vjust = -0.5, size = 3) +
  
  theme_light(base_size = 15) +  # Use a light theme
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, hjust = 1, size = 10),  # Rotate x-axis text and adjust size
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10),  # Adjust y-axis text size
        legend.position = "none") +  # No legend needed since we're showing totals
  labs(title = "Top 20 Genes by Total Counts", x = "Genes", y = "Total Count")

# Save the plot as a high-resolution image
ggsave("top_20_gene_totals_plot.png", plot = q, width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 300)

# Print the plot
print(q)

# Save data as CSV
write.csv(top_gene_totals, file = "top_gene_totals.csv", row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(resistance_counts, file = "amr.csv", row.names = FALSE)


```

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(viridis)  # For color palette

# Count occurrences of each resistance type for each gene
resistance_counts <- final_df %>%
  group_by(GENE, RESISTANCE, PRODUCT, ACCESSION, COUNTRY) %>%
  summarise(count = n(), .groups = 'drop')

# Filter out empty and NA resistance types
resistance_counts <- resistance_counts %>%
  filter(RESISTANCE != "" & !is.na(RESISTANCE))

# Summing the counts for each GENE across different RESISTANCE types
gene_totals <- resistance_counts %>%
  group_by(GENE) %>%
  summarise(total_count = sum(count), .groups = 'drop') %>%
  arrange(desc(total_count))  # Sort by total counts

# Calculate the percentage for each gene relative to the total
gene_totals <- gene_totals %>%
  mutate(percentage = (total_count / sum(total_count)) * 100)

# Get the top 20 genes with the highest percentages
top_gene_totals <- gene_totals %>%
  slice_head(n = 20)  # Select the top 20 genes

# Plotting the top 20 genes with percentages
q <- ggplot(top_gene_totals, aes(x = reorder(GENE, percentage), y = percentage)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = viridis(20)[1]) +  # Use the viridis color palette
  
  # Add labels for percentages
  geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.1f%%", percentage)), 
            vjust = -0.5, size = 4, color = "black") +
  
  # Improve the theme and visual appeal
  theme_minimal(base_size = 16) +  # Minimal theme for clean look
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 1, hjust = 1, size = 12),  # Angle x-axis labels
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12),  # Adjust y-axis text size
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 14),  # Adjust x-axis title size
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 14),  # Adjust y-axis title size
    plot.title = element_text(size = 18, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),  # Title customization
    legend.position = "none",  # No legend needed
    plot.margin = margin(1, 1, 1, 1, "cm")  # Add some padding around the plot
  ) +
  labs(title = "Top 20 AMR Genes ", x = "Genes", y = "Percentage (%)") +
  ylim(0, max(top_gene_totals$percentage) * 1.1)  # Ensure enough space for labels

# Save the plot as a high-resolution image (300 dpi)
ggsave("Result_AMR/top_20_AMR_gene_totals_plot_percentage.jpg", plot = q, width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 300)

# Print the plot
print(q)

# Save data as CSV
write.csv(top_gene_totals, file = "top_gene_totals_percentage.csv", row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(resistance_counts, file = "amr.csv", row.names = FALSE)

```

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

# Count occurrences and calculate total percentage per accession
gene_country_summary <- final_df %>%
  group_by(COUNTRY, GENE) %>%
  summarise(Count = n(), .groups = 'drop') %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(Total_Count = sum(Count),  # Total count for percentage conversion
         Percentage = (Count / Total_Count) * 100)  # Convert to percentage

# Select the top 20 most abundant accessions across all countries
top_20_accessions <- gene_country_summary %>%
  group_by(GENE) %>%
  summarise(Total_Abundance = sum(Count), .groups = 'drop') %>%
  arrange(desc(Total_Abundance)) %>%
  slice_head(n = 20) %>%
  pull(GENE)

# Filter data for only the top 20 accessions
gene_top_20 <- gene_country_summary %>%
  filter(GENE %in% top_20_accessions)

# Plot the top 20 plasmid genes as percentages
p <-ggplot(gene_top_20, aes(x = reorder(GENE, -Percentage), y = Percentage, fill = COUNTRY)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge(width = 0.8), width = 0.7) +  # Adjust bar width and spacing
  
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set2") +  # Use colorblind-friendly palette
  labs(title = "Top 20 AMR Gene Distributions by Country",
       x = "AMR Gene",
       y = "Percentage (%)",
       fill = "Country") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +  # Use clean minimal theme
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, vjust = 1),  # Improve x-axis readability
        legend.position = "top",  # Move legend to the top
        plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5))  # Center and bold title
ggsave("Result_AMR/top_20_AMR_gene_distributions.jpg", plot = p, width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 600)
print(p)
```


```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(RColorBrewer)

# Remove metadata from genes using regex
final_df$GENE <- gsub("\t.*", "", final_df$GENE)

# Create output directory for high-quality plots
output_dir <- "Result_AMR/AMR_Gene_PieCharts"
if (!dir.exists(output_dir)) dir.create(output_dir)

# Summarize AMR gene counts per country
amr_summary <- final_df %>%
  group_by(COUNTRY, GENE) %>%
  summarise(Count = n(), .groups = "drop")

# Get a unique list of genes and countries
unique_genes <- unique(amr_summary$GENE)
countries <- unique(amr_summary$COUNTRY)

# Generate a distinct color palette for all genes
num_genes <- length(unique_genes)
color_palette <- colorRampPalette(brewer.pal(12, "Set3"))(num_genes)  # Expands beyond 12 colors
gene_colors <- setNames(color_palette, unique_genes)  # Ensure consistent colors across plots

# Loop through each country and generate a pie chart
for (country in countries) {
  
  # Filter data for the specific country
  country_data <- amr_summary %>% filter(COUNTRY == country)
  
  # Generate the pie chart
  p <- ggplot(country_data, aes(x = "", y = Count, fill = GENE)) +
    geom_bar(stat = "identity", width = 1) +
    coord_polar(theta = "y") +
    theme_void() + # Remove background
    labs(title = paste("AMR Gene Abundance in", country), fill = "AMR Gene") +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 16, face = "bold"),
      legend.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
      legend.text = element_text(size = 10)
    ) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = gene_colors)  # Apply dynamically generated colors

  # Save the plot in high resolution
  ggsave(
    filename = file.path(output_dir, paste0("AMR_Genes_", country, ".jpg")),
    plot = p,
    width = 10, height = 10, dpi = 600
  )
}
p

```





```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(ggpubr)

# Summarize AMR gene counts per country
amr_summary <- final_df %>%
  group_by(COUNTRY, GENE) %>%
  summarise(Count = n(), .groups = "drop")

# Perform Kruskal-Wallis test for overall differences across countries
kruskal_test <- kruskal.test(Count ~ COUNTRY, data = amr_summary)
print(kruskal_test)

# Store p-values for pairwise Wilcoxon test
p_values <- data.frame(Comparison = character(), P_Value = numeric(), Significance = character())

if (kruskal_test$p.value < 0.05) {
  pairwise_tests <- pairwise.wilcox.test(amr_summary$Count, amr_summary$COUNTRY, p.adjust.method = "BH")
  
  for (i in 1:nrow(pairwise_tests$p.value)) {
    for (j in 1:ncol(pairwise_tests$p.value)) {
      p_val <- pairwise_tests$p.value[i, j]
      
      if (!is.na(p_val)) {
        comparison <- c(rownames(pairwise_tests$p.value)[i], colnames(pairwise_tests$p.value)[j])
        
        # Define significance levels
        sig_level <- ifelse(p_val < 0.0001, "****",
                     ifelse(p_val < 0.001, "***",
                     ifelse(p_val < 0.01, "**",
                     ifelse(p_val < 0.05, "*", "ns"))))

        # Store values
        p_values <- rbind(p_values, data.frame(Comparison = list(comparison), P_Value = p_val, Significance = sig_level))
      }
    }
  }
}

print(p_values)  # Show the p-values and significance levels

# Boxplot with significance annotations
p <- ggplot(amr_summary, aes(x = COUNTRY, y = Count, fill = COUNTRY)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, alpha = 0.6) +  # Boxplot without outliers
  geom_jitter(position = position_jitter(0.2), size = 2, alpha = 0.8) +  # Add points for individual data
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set1") +  # Color-blind friendly palette
  theme_minimal() +  
  labs(title = "AMR Gene Abundance Across Countries", y = "AMR Gene Count", x = "Country") +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 16, face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 12),
    legend.position = "none"
  )

# Add significance levels to the boxplot
if (nrow(p_values) > 0) {
  p <- p + stat_pvalue_manual(p_values, label = "Significance")
}

# Save the high-quality plot
ggsave("Result_AMR/AMR_Abundance_Comparison.jpg", plot = p, width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 600)

print(p)  # Show the plot

```

```{r}
# Summarize the frequency and percentage of each plasmid replicon (GENE)
plasmid_summary <- final_df %>%
  group_by(GENE) %>%
  summarise(Count = n(), .groups = 'drop') %>%
  mutate(Percentage = (Count / sum(Count)) * 100)

# Sort by frequency in descending order
virulence_summary <- plasmid_summary[order(-plasmid_summary$Count), ]

# View the summary
print(virulence_summary)
write.csv("Result_AMR/AMR_genes.csv", row.names = TRUE )
```


## Virulence genes
```{r}
setwd("D:/mrsa/abricate/Virulence//")
# List all summary.txt files in the directory
file_list <- list.files(pattern = "summary\\.txt$", full.names = TRUE)

# Initialize an empty list to store data frames
df_list <- list()

# Define the expected column names
common_cols <- c("FILE", "SEQUENCE", "START", "END", "STRAND", "GENE", 
                 "COVERAGE", "COVERAGE_MAP", "GAPS", "%COVERAGE", 
                 "%IDENTITY", "DATABASE", "ACCESSION", "PRODUCT", "RESISTANCE")

# Loop through each file and read it into a data frame
for (file in file_list) {
  # Check if the file is not empty
  if (file.info(file)$size > 0) {
    # Try to read the file and skip if there's an error
    try({
      df <- read.table(file, header = TRUE, sep = "\t", fill = TRUE, comment.char = "", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
      
      # Ensure that we have at least the expected number of columns (to avoid NAs)
      if (ncol(df) >= length(common_cols)) {
        # Subset the dataframe to keep only the expected columns, or add missing ones
        colnames(df) <- common_cols[1:ncol(df)]
        missing_cols <- setdiff(common_cols, colnames(df))
        
        # Add missing columns with NA values if necessary
        if (length(missing_cols) > 0) {
          df[missing_cols] <- NA
        }
        
        # Reorder columns to match the common structure
        df <- df[, common_cols]
        
        # Store each valid data frame in the list
        df_list[[file]] <- df
      } else {
        cat("Skipping file due to insufficient columns:", file, "\n")
      }
    }, silent = TRUE)
  } else {
    cat("Skipping empty file:", file, "\n")
  }
}

# Combine all valid data frames into one
final_df <- do.call(rbind, df_list)

# Optionally, you can write the combined dataframe to a new file
write.csv(final_df, "merged_summary.csv", row.names = TRUE)

# Print the combined data frame to check
print(final_df)

```


```{r}
# Extract genome IDs from the FILE column
final_df$GENOME_ID <- gsub(".*(ERR[0-9]+).*", "\\1", final_df$FILE)

# Define genome ID ranges for each country
uganda_ids <- paste0("ERR", 4692235:4692276)
kenya_ids <- c(
  paste0("ERR", 3150908:3151001), "ERR3218227",
  paste0("ERR", 1764887:1765052),
  paste0("ERR", 1791869:1791882),
  paste0("ERR", 2436451:2436455))
tanzania_ids <- c(
  paste0("ERR", 12916291:12916300),
  paste0("ERR", 12511686:12511850))

# Create a mapping dataframe
country_mapping <- data.frame(
  GENOME_ID = c(uganda_ids, kenya_ids, tanzania_ids),
  COUNTRY = c(
    rep("Uganda", length(uganda_ids)),
    rep("Kenya", length(kenya_ids)),
    rep("Tanzania", length(tanzania_ids))
  )
)

# Merge country information into final_df
final_df <- merge(final_df, country_mapping, by = "GENOME_ID", all.x = TRUE)

# View the updated dataframe
head(final_df)
```




```{r}
# Set working directory
setwd("D:/mrsa/abricate/Virulence/")

# Load necessary libraries
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(viridis)  # For color palette

# Count occurrences of each resistance type for each gene
resistance_counts <- final_df %>%
  group_by(GENE, PRODUCT, ACCESSION) %>%
  summarise(count = n(), .groups = 'drop')

# Summing the counts for each GENE across different RESISTANCE types
gene_totals <- resistance_counts %>%
  group_by(GENE) %>%
  summarise(total_count = sum(count), .groups = 'drop') %>%
  arrange(desc(total_count))  # Sort by total counts

# Calculate overall total counts for percentage calculation
overall_total <- sum(gene_totals$total_count)

# Calculate percentages
gene_totals <- gene_totals %>%
  mutate(percentage = (total_count / overall_total) * 100)

# Get the top 20 genes with the highest overall percentages
top_gene_totals <- gene_totals %>%
  slice_head(n = 20)  # Select the top 20 genes

# Plotting the top 20 genes with percentages
q <- ggplot(top_gene_totals, aes(x = reorder(GENE, percentage), y = percentage)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = viridis(1), color = "black") +  # Add border color to bars
  
  # Add labels for percentages with adjustment
  geom_text(aes(label = paste0(round(percentage, 1), "%")), 
            vjust = -0.5, size = 4, color = "black") +  # Adjust the label to show percentage
  
  theme_light(base_size = 15) +  # Use a light theme
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, hjust = 1, size = 12),  # Rotate x-axis text and adjust size
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12),  # Adjust y-axis text size
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 14),  # Adjust x-axis title size
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 14),  # Adjust y-axis title size
    plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"),  # Title customization
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 12, face = "italic"),  # Subtitle customization
    legend.position = "none",  # No legend needed since we're showing totals
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "grey80"),  # Major grid line color
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()  # Remove minor grid lines
  ) + 
  labs(
    title = "Top 20 S.aureus Virulent Genes in East Africa",
    subtitle = "",
    x = "Genes",
    y = "(%)"
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(scale = 1)) +  # Format y-axis as percentage
  ylim(0, max(top_gene_totals$percentage) * 1.1)  # Set y-axis limits for better visualization

# Save the plot as a high-resolution image
ggsave("top_20_gene_percentages_plot_improved.png", plot = q, width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 300)

# Print the plot
print(q)

# Save data as CSV
write.csv(top_gene_totals, file = "top_gene_percentages.csv", row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(resistance_counts, file = "amr.csv", row.names = FALSE)

```


```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

# Count occurrences and calculate total percentage per accession
gene_country_summary <- final_df %>%
  group_by(COUNTRY, GENE) %>%
  summarise(Count = n(), .groups = 'drop') %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(Total_Count = sum(Count),  # Total count for percentage conversion
         Percentage = (Count / Total_Count) * 100)  # Convert to percentage

# Select the top 20 most abundant accessions across all countries
top_20_accessions <- gene_country_summary %>%
  group_by(GENE) %>%
  summarise(Total_Abundance = sum(Count), .groups = 'drop') %>%
  arrange(desc(Total_Abundance)) %>%
  slice_head(n = 20) %>%
  pull(GENE)

# Filter data for only the top 20 accessions
gene_top_20 <- gene_country_summary %>%
  filter(GENE %in% top_20_accessions)

# Plot the top 20 plasmid genes as percentages
p <-ggplot(gene_top_20, aes(x = reorder(GENE, -Percentage), y = Percentage, fill = COUNTRY)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge(width = 0.8), width = 0.7) +  # Adjust bar width and spacing
  
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set2") +  # Use colorblind-friendly palette
  labs(title = "Top 20 Virulence Gene Distributions by Country",
       x = "Virulence Gene",
       y = "Percentage (%)",
       fill = "Country") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +  # Use clean minimal theme
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, vjust = 1),  # Improve x-axis readability
        legend.position = "top",  # Move legend to the top
        plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5))  # Center and bold title
ggsave("Result_virulence/top_20_virulence_gene_distributions.jpg", plot = p, width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 600)
print(p)
```

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(RColorBrewer)

# Remove metadata from genes using regex
final_df$GENE <- gsub("\t.*", "", final_df$GENE)

# Create output directory for high-quality plots
output_dir <- "Result_virulence/virulence_Gene_PieCharts"
if (!dir.exists(output_dir)) dir.create(output_dir)

# Summarize AMR gene counts per country
amr_summary <- final_df %>%
  group_by(COUNTRY, GENE) %>%
  summarise(Count = n(), .groups = "drop")

# Get a unique list of genes and countries
unique_genes <- unique(amr_summary$GENE)
countries <- unique(amr_summary$COUNTRY)

# Generate a distinct color palette for all genes
num_genes <- length(unique_genes)
color_palette <- colorRampPalette(brewer.pal(12, "Set3"))(num_genes)  # Expands beyond 12 colors
gene_colors <- setNames(color_palette, unique_genes)  # Ensure consistent colors across plots

# Loop through each country and generate a pie chart
for (country in countries) {
  
  # Filter data for the specific country
  country_data <- amr_summary %>% filter(COUNTRY == country)
  
  # Generate the pie chart
  p <- ggplot(country_data, aes(x = "", y = Count, fill = GENE)) +
    geom_bar(stat = "identity", width = 1) +
    coord_polar(theta = "y") +
    theme_void() + # Remove background
    labs(title = paste("Virulence Gene Abundance in", country), fill = "Virulence Gene") +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 16, face = "bold"),
      legend.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
      legend.text = element_text(size = 10)
    ) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = gene_colors)  # Apply dynamically generated colors

  # Save the plot in high resolution
  ggsave(
    filename = file.path(output_dir, paste0("Virulence_Genes_", country, ".jpg")),
    plot = p,
    width = 12, height = 10, dpi = 600
  )
}
```

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(ggpubr)

# Check if COUNTRY is a factor
amr_summary$COUNTRY <- as.factor(amr_summary$COUNTRY)

# Summarize AMR gene counts per country
amr_summary <- final_df %>%
  group_by(COUNTRY, GENE) %>%
  summarise(Count = n(), .groups = "drop")


# Convert 'COUNTRY' column to a factor to ensure it's treated as categorical
amr_summary$COUNTRY <- as.factor(amr_summary$COUNTRY)

# Create a boxplot to visualize AMR gene abundance by country
boxplot <- ggplot(amr_summary, aes(x = COUNTRY, y = Count, fill = COUNTRY)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, alpha = 0.6) +  # Add boxplot without outliers for better readability
  labs(title = "Virulence Gene Abundance by Country",
       x = "Country",
       y = "Virulence Gene Count") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 14, face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 10),
    legend.position = "none"
  )

# Add statistical significance annotations for Kruskal-Wallis test
boxplot <- boxplot + stat_compare_means(
  method = "kruskal.test",  # Use Kruskal-Wallis test for non-parametric data
  label = "p.signif",       # Show significance as asterisks
  label.x = 1.5,            # Adjust label position on the x-axis
  label.y = max(amr_summary$Count) * 1.1  # Place label above the plot, adjust based on your data
)

# Add pairwise comparisons with asterisks (you can modify the groups based on your data)
boxplot <- boxplot + stat_compare_means(
  comparisons = list(c("Tanzania", "Kenya"), c("Tanzania", "Uganda"), c("Kenya", "Uganda")),
  method = "wilcox.test",  # Use Wilcoxon test for pairwise comparisons
  label = "p.signif",      # Show significance as asterisks
  tip.length = 0.01        # Adjust the length of the comparison lines
)

# Display the plot
print(boxplot)

# Save the plot as a high-quality image
ggsave("Result_virulence/Virulence_gene_abundance_boxplot.png", boxplot, width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 600, bg = "white")

```

```{r}
# Summarize the frequency and percentage of each plasmid replicon (GENE)
plasmid_summary <- final_df %>%
  group_by(GENE) %>%
  summarise(Count = n(), .groups = 'drop') %>%
  mutate(Percentage = (Count / sum(Count)) * 100)

# Sort by frequency in descending order
virulence_summary <- plasmid_summary[order(-plasmid_summary$Count), ]

# View the summary
print(virulence_summary)
write.csv("Result_virulence/virulence_genes.csv", row.names = TRUE )
```

```{r}
# Load required libraries
library(tidyverse)

# STEP 1: Create presence/absence matrix
presence_matrix <- final_df %>%
  select(GENOME_ID, GENE) %>%
  distinct() %>%
  mutate(present = 1) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = GENE, values_from = present, values_fill = 0)

# Extract gene columns only
gene_matrix <- presence_matrix %>% column_to_rownames("GENOME_ID")

# STEP 2 & 3: Run Fisherâ€™s Exact Test for all gene pairs
gene_names <- colnames(gene_matrix)
results <- list()

for (i in 1:(length(gene_names)-1)) {
  for (j in (i+1):length(gene_names)) {
    gene1 <- gene_names[i]
    gene2 <- gene_names[j]
    
    tab <- table(gene_matrix[[gene1]], gene_matrix[[gene2]])
    
    if (all(dim(tab) == c(2,2))) {
      fisher <- fisher.test(tab)
      
      results[[length(results)+1]] <- data.frame(
        gene1 = gene1,
        gene2 = gene2,
        p_value = fisher$p.value,
        odds_ratio = fisher$estimate
      )
    }
  }
}

# Combine all results
fisher_df <- bind_rows(results)

# STEP 4: Filter for significant pairs (e.g., p < 0.001)
sig_pairs <- fisher_df %>%
  filter(p_value < 0.001 & odds_ratio > 1) %>% 
  arrange(p_value)

# View top co-occurring gene pairs
print(sig_pairs)
write.csv(sig_pairs, "virulence_networks/network.csv")
```









## Plasmids
```{r}
setwd("D:/mrsa/abricate/Plasmids//")
# List all summary.txt files in the directory
file_list <- list.files(pattern = "summary\\.txt$", full.names = TRUE)

# Initialize an empty list to store data frames
df_list <- list()

# Define the expected column names
common_cols <- c("FILE", "SEQUENCE", "START", "END", "STRAND", "GENE", 
                 "COVERAGE", "COVERAGE_MAP", "GAPS", "%COVERAGE", 
                 "%IDENTITY", "DATABASE", "ACCESSION", "PRODUCT")

# Loop through each file and read it into a data frame
for (file in file_list) {
  # Check if the file is not empty
  if (file.info(file)$size > 0) {
    # Try to read the file and skip if there's an error
    try({
      df <- read.table(file, header = TRUE, sep = "\t", fill = TRUE, comment.char = "", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
      
      # Ensure that we have at least the expected number of columns (to avoid NAs)
      if (ncol(df) >= length(common_cols)) {
        # Subset the dataframe to keep only the expected columns, or add missing ones
        colnames(df) <- common_cols[1:ncol(df)]
        missing_cols <- setdiff(common_cols, colnames(df))
        
        # Add missing columns with NA values if necessary
        if (length(missing_cols) > 0) {
          df[missing_cols] <- NA
        }
        
        # Reorder columns to match the common structure
        df <- df[, common_cols]
        
        # Store each valid data frame in the list
        df_list[[file]] <- df
      } else {
        cat("Skipping file due to insufficient columns:", file, "\n")
      }
    }, silent = TRUE)
  } else {
    cat("Skipping empty file:", file, "\n")
  }
}

# Combine all valid data frames into one
final_df <- do.call(rbind, df_list)

# Optionally, you can write the combined dataframe to a new file
write.csv(final_df, "merged_summary.csv", row.names = TRUE)

# Print the combined data frame to check
print(final_df)

```

```{r}
# Extract genome IDs from the FILE column
final_df$GENOME_ID <- gsub(".*(ERR[0-9]+).*", "\\1", final_df$FILE)

# Define genome ID ranges for each country
uganda_ids <- paste0("ERR", 4692235:4692276)
kenya_ids <- c(
  paste0("ERR", 3150908:3151001), "ERR3218227",
  paste0("ERR", 1764887:1765052),
  paste0("ERR", 1791869:1791882),
  paste0("ERR", 2436451:2436455))
tanzania_ids <- c(
  paste0("ERR", 12916291:12916300),
  paste0("ERR", 12511686:12511850))

# Create a mapping dataframe
country_mapping <- data.frame(
  GENOME_ID = c(uganda_ids, kenya_ids, tanzania_ids),
  COUNTRY = c(
    rep("Uganda", length(uganda_ids)),
    rep("Kenya", length(kenya_ids)),
    rep("Tanzania", length(tanzania_ids))
  )
)

# Merge country information into final_df
final_df <- merge(final_df, country_mapping, by = "GENOME_ID", all.x = TRUE)

# View the updated dataframe
head(final_df)
```
```{r}
final_df <- final_df %>% select( GENOME_ID, GENE, ACCESSION, PRODUCT, COUNTRY)
```

```{r}
# Count the occurrence of each gene by country
gene_country_summary <- final_df %>%
  group_by(COUNTRY, ACCESSION) %>%
  summarise(Count = n(), .groups = 'drop')

# View the summary
print(gene_country_summary)
```


```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
# Count occurrences and calculate total percentage per accession
gene_country_summary <- final_df %>%
  group_by(COUNTRY, ACCESSION) %>%
  summarise(Count = n(), .groups = 'drop') %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(Total_Count = sum(Count),  # Total count for percentage conversion
         Percentage = (Count / Total_Count) * 100)  # Convert to percentage

# Select the top 20 most abundant accessions across all countries
top_20_accessions <- gene_country_summary %>%
  group_by(ACCESSION) %>%
  summarise(Total_Abundance = sum(Count), .groups = 'drop') %>%
  arrange(desc(Total_Abundance)) %>%
  slice_head(n = 20) %>%
  pull(ACCESSION)

# Filter data for only the top 20 accessions
gene_top_20 <- gene_country_summary %>%
  filter(ACCESSION %in% top_20_accessions)

# Create the plot
p <- ggplot(gene_top_20, aes(x = reorder(ACCESSION, -Percentage), y = Percentage, fill = COUNTRY)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge(width = 0.8), width = 0.7) +  # Adjust bar width and spacing
  
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set2") +  # Use colorblind-friendly palette
  labs(title = "Top 20 Plasmid Gene Distributions by Country",
       x = "Plasmid Accession",
       y = "Percentage (%)",
       fill = "Country") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +  # Use clean minimal theme
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, vjust = 1),  # Improve x-axis readability
        legend.position = "top",  # Move legend to the top
        plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5))  # Center and bold title

# Save the plot as a high-resolution image (600 DPI)
ggsave("Results/top_20_plasmid_accession_distributions.jpg", plot = p, width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 600)

# Print the plot to the screen
print(p)

```


```{r}
# Dominant replicons per country  
country_dominance <- gene_top_20 %>%  
  group_by(COUNTRY) %>%  
  slice_max(Percentage, n = 3)  

# Co-occurrence calculation (example for Tanzania)  
tanzania_multi_replicon <- final_df %>%  
  filter(COUNTRY == "Tanzania") %>%  
  group_by(GENOME_ID) %>%  
  summarise(N_Replicons = n_distinct(GENE)) %>%  
  filter(N_Replicons >= 3) %>%  
  nrow() / n_distinct(filter(final_df, COUNTRY == "Tanzania")$GENOME_ID) * 100  
```


```{r}
library(igraph)
library(ggraph)
library(tidygraph)

# Create a binary matrix of replicons per genome
binary_matrix <- final_df %>%
  distinct(GENOME_ID, GENE) %>%  # Remove duplicate replicons per genome
  mutate(value = 1) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = GENE, values_from = value, values_fill = 0) %>%
  column_to_rownames("GENOME_ID")

# Calculate co-occurrence (Jaccard similarity)
co_occurrence <- as.matrix(binary_matrix) %*% t(binary_matrix)  # Dot product
total_genomes <- rowSums(binary_matrix)
jaccard_matrix <- co_occurrence / (outer(total_genomes, total_genomes, "+") - co_occurrence)

# Convert to edge list (filter weak connections)
edge_list <- jaccard_matrix %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  rownames_to_column("Replicon_A") %>%
  pivot_longer(-Replicon_A, names_to = "Replicon_B", values_to = "Jaccard") %>%
  filter(Jaccard > 0.2 & Replicon_A != Replicon_B)  # Threshold for edges

# Create network graph
network_graph <- as_tbl_graph(edge_list, directed = FALSE) %>%
  activate(nodes) %>%
  mutate(
    Degree = centrality_degree(),
    Community = as.factor(group_louvain())
  )

# Plot
set.seed(123)  # For reproducibility
ggraph(network_graph, layout = "fr") +  # Fruchterman-Reingold layout
  geom_edge_link(aes(width = Jaccard, alpha = Jaccard), color = "gray50") +
  geom_node_point(aes(size = Degree, color = Community), show.legend = TRUE) +
  geom_node_text(aes(label = name), repel = TRUE, size = 3) +
  scale_edge_width(range = c(0.5, 3)) +
  scale_size(range = c(3, 10)) +
  labs(title = "Co-occurrence Network of Plasmid Replicons",
       subtitle = "Edge width/opacity = Jaccard similarity; Node size = Degree") +
  theme_graph(base_family = "sans") +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

ggsave("Results/plasmid_replicon_network.jpg", width = 10, height = 8, dpi = 600)
```





```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

# Count occurrences and calculate total percentage per accession
gene_country_summary <- final_df %>%
  group_by(COUNTRY, GENE) %>%
  summarise(Count = n(), .groups = 'drop') %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(Total_Count = sum(Count),  # Total count for percentage conversion
         Percentage = (Count / Total_Count) * 100)  # Convert to percentage

# Select the top 20 most abundant accessions across all countries
top_20_accessions <- gene_country_summary %>%
  group_by(GENE) %>%
  summarise(Total_Abundance = sum(Count), .groups = 'drop') %>%
  arrange(desc(Total_Abundance)) %>%
  slice_head(n = 20) %>%
  pull(GENE)

# Filter data for only the top 20 accessions
gene_top_20 <- gene_country_summary %>%
  filter(GENE %in% top_20_accessions)

# Plot the top 20 plasmid genes as percentages
p <-ggplot(gene_top_20, aes(x = reorder(GENE, -Percentage), y = Percentage, fill = COUNTRY)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge(width = 0.8), width = 0.7) +  # Adjust bar width and spacing
  
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set2") +  # Use colorblind-friendly palette
  labs(title = "Top 20 Plasmid Gene Distributions by Country",
       x = "Plasmid Gene",
       y = "Percentage (%)",
       fill = "Country") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +  # Use clean minimal theme
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, vjust = 1),  # Improve x-axis readability
        legend.position = "top",  # Move legend to the top
        plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5))  # Center and bold title
ggsave("Results/top_20_plasmid_gene_distributions.jpg", plot = p, width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 600)
print(p)
```

```{r}
# Count the occurrence of each gene by country
gene_country_summary <- final_df %>%
  group_by(COUNTRY, PRODUCT) %>%
  summarise(Count = n(), .groups = 'drop')

# View the summary
print(gene_country_summary)
```



```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(reshape2)
library(pheatmap)
library(viridis)  # Better color palette

# Reshape data for heatmap
gene_country_matrix <- dcast(gene_country_summary, PRODUCT ~ COUNTRY, value.var = "Count", fill = 0)

# Convert to matrix for heatmap
heatmap_data <- as.matrix(gene_country_matrix[, -1])  # Exclude GENE column
rownames(heatmap_data) <- gene_country_matrix$PRODUCT  

# Get top 50 most abundant genes
top_genes <- names(sort(rowSums(heatmap_data), decreasing = TRUE)[1:50])
heatmap_data_filtered <- heatmap_data[top_genes, ]

# Scale the data by row and replace NaN values with 0
heatmap_data_scaled <- t(scale(t(heatmap_data_filtered)))  
heatmap_data_scaled[is.na(heatmap_data_scaled)] <- 0  

# Save the heatmap as a high-resolution image (300 DPI)
png("Results/top_50_plasmid_genes_by_country_heatmap.jpg", width = 10, height = 8, units = "in", res = 300)

# Plot the heatmap
pheatmap(heatmap_data_scaled, 
         clustering_distance_rows = "euclidean",
         clustering_distance_cols = "euclidean",
         clustering_method = "complete",
         color = viridis(100),  # New color palette
         main = "Top 50 Plasmid Genes by Country",
         fontsize_row = 10,  # Bigger text for better readability
         fontsize_col = 12,
         border_color = "white",
         angle_col = 45)

# Turn off the device to save the plot
dev.off()


```




```{r}
# Create a contingency table
contingency_table <- table(final_df$GENE, final_df$COUNTRY)

# Perform Chi-Square test
chi_square_test <- chisq.test(contingency_table)
print(chi_square_test)
```

```{r}
# Filter for a specific gene (e.g., rep5a_1_repSAP001(pN315))
specific_gene <- final_df %>% filter(GENE == "rep5a_1_repSAP001(pN315)")

# Count occurrences by country
specific_gene_summary <- specific_gene %>%
  group_by(COUNTRY) %>%
  summarise(Count = n(), .groups = 'drop')

# Bar plot for the specific gene
ggplot(specific_gene_summary, aes(x = COUNTRY, y = Count, fill = COUNTRY)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  labs(title = "Distribution of rep5a_1_repSAP001(pN315) by Country",
       x = "Country",
       y = "Count")
```





```{r}
# Load necessary libraries
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(patchwork)  # For arranging multiple plots

# Summarize plasmid gene distribution for each country
country_genes <- final_df %>%
  group_by(COUNTRY, GENE) %>%
  summarise(Count = n(), .groups = 'drop')

# Create a function to generate a pie chart for a specific country
create_pie_chart <- function(country_data, country_name) {
  ggplot(country_data, aes(x = "", y = Count, fill = GENE)) +
    geom_bar(stat = "identity", width = 1, color = "white") +
    coord_polar("y", start = 0) +
    labs(title = paste("Plasmid Gene Distribution in", country_name),
         fill = "Plasmid Gene") +
    theme_void() +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 14, face = "bold"),
      legend.position = "right",
      legend.text = element_text(size = 10),
      legend.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold")
    )
}

# Filter data for each country
tanzania_data <- country_genes %>% filter(COUNTRY == "Tanzania")
kenya_data <- country_genes %>% filter(COUNTRY == "Kenya")
uganda_data <- country_genes %>% filter(COUNTRY == "Uganda")

# Generate pie charts for each country
tanzania_pie <- create_pie_chart(tanzania_data, "Tanzania")
kenya_pie <- create_pie_chart(kenya_data, "Kenya")
uganda_pie <- create_pie_chart(uganda_data, "Uganda")
uganda_pie
tanzania_pie
kenya_pie

# Arrange the three pie charts in a single figure
combined_plot <- (tanzania_pie | kenya_pie | uganda_pie) +
  plot_layout(guides = "collect")  # Combine legends


combined_plot
ggsave("Results/plasmid_gene_distribution_Uganda_pie_charts.jpg", uganda_pie,
       width = 12, height = 6, dpi = 600, bg = "white")
ggsave("Results/plasmid_gene_distribution_Kenya_pie_charts.jpg", kenya_pie,
       width = 12, height = 6, dpi = 600, bg = "white")
ggsave("Results/plasmid_gene_distribution_Tanzania_pie_charts.jpg", tanzania_pie,
       width = 12, height = 6, dpi = 600, bg = "white")
```


```{r}
# Load necessary libraries
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

# Create the plot
tanzania_genes <- final_df %>% filter(COUNTRY == "Kenya") %>%
  group_by(GENE) %>%
  summarise(Count = n(), .groups = 'drop')

plot <- ggplot(tanzania_genes, aes(x = "", y = Count, fill = GENE)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", width = 1) +
  coord_polar("y", start = 0) +
  labs(title = "Plasmid Gene Distribution in Kenya") +
  theme_void() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "right",   # Adjust position of the legend
    legend.key.size = unit(0.5, "cm"),  # Reduce legend key size
    legend.text = element_text(size = 8),  # Reduce legend text size
    plot.margin = margin(0, 2, 0, 0, "cm")  # Adjust plot margins for better space distribution
  )

# Save the plot as a high resolution image (300 dpi)
ggsave("Kenya_plasmid_gene_distribution.jpg", plot = plot, width = 15, height = 8, dpi = 600)

# Print plot to screen as well
print(plot)


```



```{r}
# Create a co-occurrence matrix
co_occurrence_matrix <- crossprod(table(final_df$GENOME_ID, final_df$GENE))

# Plot the network
library(igraph)

# Convert co-occurrence matrix to a graph
gene_network <- graph_from_adjacency_matrix(co_occurrence_matrix, mode = "undirected", weighted = TRUE)

# Plot the network
plot(gene_network, edge.width = E(gene_network)$weight,
     vertex.label.cex = 0.8, vertex.size = 10,
     main = "Co-occurrence Network of Plasmid Genes")
```
```{r}
# Summarize the frequency and percentage of each plasmid replicon (GENE)
plasmid_summary <- final_df %>%
  group_by(GENE) %>%
  summarise(Count = n(), .groups = 'drop') %>%
  mutate(Percentage = (Count / sum(Count)) * 100)

# Sort by frequency in descending order
plasmid_summary <- plasmid_summary[order(-plasmid_summary$Count), ]

# View the summary
print(plasmid_summary)
write.csv("Results/Plasmid_genes.csv", row.names = TRUE )

```


```{r}
# Count the number of plasmids per strain
plasmids_per_strain <- final_df %>%
  group_by(GENOME_ID) %>%
  summarise(Plasmid_Count = n(), .groups = 'drop')

# Find the maximum number of plasmids per strain
max_plasmids <- max(plasmids_per_strain$Plasmid_Count)

# Count how many strains have the maximum number of plasmids
strains_with_max <- sum(plasmids_per_strain$Plasmid_Count == max_plasmids)

ave_plasmids <- mean(plasmids_per_strain$Plasmid_Count)

# View the results
cat("Maximum number of plasmids per strain:", max_plasmids, "\n")
cat("Number of strains with the maximum:", strains_with_max, "\n")

ave_plasmids
```

```{r}
# Filter strains with the maximum number of plasmids
strains_max_plasmids <- plasmids_per_strain %>%
  filter(Plasmid_Count == max_plasmids)

# View the strains
print(strains_max_plasmids)
```





```{r}
setwd("D:/mrsa/abricate/Plasmids//")
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(viridis)  # For color palette

# Count occurrences of each resistance type for each gene
resistance_counts <- final_df %>%
  group_by(GENE, ACCESSION) %>%
  summarise(count = n(), .groups = 'drop')

# Summing the counts for each GENE across different RESISTANCE types
gene_totals <- resistance_counts %>%
  group_by(GENE) %>%
  summarise(total_count = sum(count), .groups = 'drop') %>%
  arrange(desc(total_count))  # Sort by total counts

# Get the top 20 genes with the highest overall counts
top_gene_totals <- gene_totals %>%
  slice_head(n = 20)  # Select the top 20 genes

# Plotting the top 20 genes with total counts
q <- ggplot(top_gene_totals, aes(x = reorder(GENE, total_count), y = total_count)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = viridis(20)[1], width = 0.7) +  # Use viridis color
  geom_text(aes(label = total_count), 
            vjust = -0.5, size = 4, color = "black") +  # Improved label size and color
  
  theme_light(base_size = 15) +  # Use a light theme
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, hjust = 1, size = 12),  # Rotate x-axis text and adjust size
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12),  # Adjust y-axis text size
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 14),  # Adjust x-axis title size
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 14),  # Adjust y-axis title size
    plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"),  # Title customization
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 12, face = "italic"),  # Subtitle customization
    legend.position = "none",  # No legend needed since we're showing totals
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "grey80"),  # Major grid line color
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()  # Remove minor grid lines
  ) + 
  labs(
    title = "S.aureus Plasmid abundance",
    subtitle = "",
    x = "Plasmids",
    y = "Total Count"
  ) +
  ylim(0, max(top_gene_totals$total_count) * 1.1)  # Set y-axis limits for better visualization

# Save the plot as a high-resolution image
ggsave("top_20_gene_totals_plot_improved.png", plot = q, width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 300)




# Print the plot
print(q)

# Save data as CSV
write.csv(top_gene_totals, file = "top_gene_totals.csv", row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(resistance_counts, file = "amr.csv", row.names = FALSE)


```

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(viridis)

# Count occurrences of each resistance type for each gene
resistance_counts <- final_df %>%
  group_by(GENE, ACCESSION) %>%
  summarise(count = n(), .groups = 'drop')

# Summing the counts for each GENE
gene_totals <- resistance_counts %>%
  group_by(GENE) %>%
  summarise(total_count = sum(count), .groups = 'drop') %>%
  arrange(desc(total_count))

# Calculate percentages
gene_totals <- gene_totals %>%
  mutate(percentage = (total_count / sum(total_count)) * 100)

# Get the top 20 genes based on percentage
top_gene_totals <- gene_totals %>%
  slice_head(n = 20)

# Plot the top 20 genes with percentages
q <- ggplot(top_gene_totals, aes(x = reorder(GENE, percentage), y = percentage)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = viridis(20, option = "plasma")[10], width = 0.7) +
  
  # Add percentage labels
  geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.1f%%", percentage)), 
            vjust = -0.5, size = 5, color = "black") +
  
  theme_minimal(base_size = 16) +  # Use minimal theme for a clean look
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, hjust = 1, size = 14),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 14),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 18, face = "bold"),
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "grey85"),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  ) +
  
  labs(
    title = "S. aureus Plasmid Abundance",
    x = "Plasmid Genes",
    y = "Percentage (%)"
  ) +
  
  ylim(0, max(top_gene_totals$percentage) * 1.1)  # Adjust y-axis for better spacing

# Save as a high-resolution image
ggsave("Results/top_20_gene_percentage_plot.png", plot = q, width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 600)

# Print the plot
print(q)

# Save processed data
write.csv(top_gene_totals, file = "top_gene_percentages.csv", row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(resistance_counts, file = "amr.csv", row.names = FALSE)

```



```{r}
# Load necessary libraries
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggpubr)  # For adding statistical annotations to plots

# Summarize plasmid abundance (count) per strain
plasmid_abundance <- final_df %>%
  group_by(GENOME_ID, COUNTRY) %>%
  summarise(Plasmid_Count = n(), .groups = 'drop')

# View the summarized data
print(plasmid_abundance)
```

```{r}
# Check normality assumption (optional)
shapiro.test(plasmid_abundance$Plasmid_Count)  # If p > 0.05, data is normally distributed

# Perform ANOVA
anova_result <- aov(Plasmid_Count ~ COUNTRY, data = plasmid_abundance)
summary(anova_result)

# Post-hoc test (Tukey's HSD) to identify pairwise differences
tukey_result <- TukeyHSD(anova_result)
print(tukey_result)
```
```{r}
# Perform Kruskal-Wallis test
kruskal_result <- kruskal.test(Plasmid_Count ~ COUNTRY, data = plasmid_abundance)
print(kruskal_result)

# Post-hoc test (Dunn's test) to identify pairwise differences
library(dunn.test)

dunn_result <- dunn.test(plasmid_abundance$Plasmid_Count, plasmid_abundance$COUNTRY, method = "bonferroni")
print(dunn_result)
```



```{r}
# Load necessary libraries
library(ggplot2)
library(ggpubr)

# Create a boxplot to visualize plasmid abundance by country
boxplot <- ggplot(plasmid_abundance, aes(x = COUNTRY, y = Plasmid_Count, fill = COUNTRY)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "Plasmid Abundance by Country",
       x = "Country",
       y = "Plasmid Count per Strain") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 14, face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 10),
    legend.position = "none"
  )

# Add statistical significance annotations (asterisks)
boxplot <- boxplot + stat_compare_means(
  method = "kruskal.test",  # Use "anova" if ANOVA was performed
  label = "p.signif",       # Show significance as asterisks
  label.x = 1.5,            # Adjust label position
  label.y = max(plasmid_abundance$Plasmid_Count) * 1.1  # Place label above the plot
)

# Add pairwise comparisons with asterisks
boxplot <- boxplot + stat_compare_means(
  comparisons = list(c("Tanzania", "Kenya"), c("Tanzania", "Uganda"), c("Kenya", "Uganda")),
  method = "wilcox.test",  # Use "t.test" if ANOVA was performed
  label = "p.signif",      # Show significance as asterisks
  tip.length = 0.01        # Adjust the length of comparison lines
)

# Display the plot
print(boxplot)

# Save the plot as a high-quality image
ggsave("Results/plasmid_abundance_boxplot.png", boxplot, width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 600, bg = "white")
```



## MLST
###Read in files
```{r}
library(readr)      # Ensure the readr library is loaded
library(dplyr)      # Ensure dplyr is available for data manipulation
library(tidyr)      # Ensure tidyr is loaded for the separate function

setwd("../MLST")    # Set the working directory

# List all CSV files in the directory
csv_files <- list.files(pattern = "*.csv", full.names = TRUE)  # Use full.names to get complete paths

# Read and combine all tab-delimited CSV files into one dataframe
combined_df <- csv_files %>%
  lapply(read_delim, delim = "\t", col_names = FALSE) %>%  # Read tab-delimited files
  bind_rows()                                              # Combine all dataframes into one

# View the combined dataframe (just to verify it again)
print(combined_df)

# Now separate the single column into multiple columns
# Assuming your combined data is in the first column (which is the default if you read with col_names = FALSE)
combined_df <- combined_df %>%
  separate(X1, 
           into = c("path", "species", "unknown", "arcC", "aroE", "glpF", "gmk", "pta", "tpi", "yqiL"),
           sep = ",")  # Use comma as the separator

# View the separated dataframe
print(combined_df)


```

```{r}

# Step 1: Rename the `unknown` column to `ST-type`
combined_df <- combined_df %>%
  rename(`ST-type` = unknown)

# Step 2: Extract the ID (ERRxxxxxxx) from the `path` column
combined_df <- combined_df %>%
  mutate(path = str_extract(path, "ERR\\d+"))
# Replace '-' in the ST-type column with 'Unidentified'
combined_df <- combined_df %>%
  mutate(`species` = ifelse(`species` == "-", "Unidentified", `species`))
# Classify the Origin based on the first six digits in the path column
combined_df <- combined_df %>%
  mutate(Origin = case_when(
    grepl("^ERR469", path) ~ "Uganda",  # Any row with "ERR469" is Uganda
    grepl("^ERR315|^ERR321|^ERR176|^ERR179|^ERR243", path) ~ "Kenya",  # Any of these prefixes are Kenya
    grepl("^ERR129|^ERR125", path) ~ "Tanzania",  # Any of these prefixes are Tanzania
    TRUE ~ "Unknown"  # Default to "Unknown" if no matches
  ))

# Check and print the updated dataframe
print(combined_df, n = 10)
# Now, `combined_df` has the `ST-type` column and the `path` column with only the sample IDs.

```
## Figure 2
```{r}
# Load required libraries
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

# Step 1: Create a summary data frame to count occurrences of each species by Origin
species_origin_counts <- combined_df %>%
  group_by(species, Origin) %>%
  summarize(count = n(), .groups = 'drop')

# Step 2: Create an enhanced grouped bar plot
p <- ggplot(species_origin_counts, aes(x = species, y = count, fill = Origin)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "dodge") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 15) +  # Increase base font size for better readability
  labs(title = "Counts of Different Species by Origin",
       x = "Species",
       y = "Count") +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, vjust = 1),  # Rotate x-axis labels
    legend.title = element_text(size = 12),  # Increase legend title size
    legend.text = element_text(size = 10),    # Increase legend text size
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 16, face = "bold")  # Center and bold the title
  ) +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set1")  # Use a color palette for better differentiation

# Step 3: Save the plot as a high-resolution image
ggsave("species_origin_counts_plot.png", plot = p, width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 300)
p


```

```{r}

# Step 1: Filter rows where species is NOT 'saureus' and store them in a new dataframe
non_saureus_df <- combined_df %>%
  filter(species != 'saureus')

# View the non-saureus dataframe
non_saureus_df

```

```{r}
# Step 1: Remove rows where species is not 'saureus' from combined_df
combined_df <- combined_df %>%
  filter(species == 'saureus')

# View the updated combined_df with only 'saureus' species
combined_df


# Step 1: Create a new dataframe by extracting only the digits from columns 4 to 10
combined_df<- combined_df %>%
  mutate(across(arcC:yqiL, ~str_extract(., "\\d+")))
# Step 1: Replace "-" in the ST-type column with "Unique"
combined_df <- combined_df%>%
  mutate(`ST-type` = ifelse(`ST-type` == "-", "Unique", `ST-type`))

# Step 2: Convert columns arcC to yqiL to factors (ordinal data)
combined_df<- combined_df %>%
  mutate(across(arcC:yqiL, as.factor))

rownames(combined_df)<- combined_df$path
combined_df <- as.data.frame(combined_df)


# View the cleaned dataframe
head(combined_df)

```



##MLST bar plot
```{r}
# Load necessary libraries
library(ggplot2)
library(viridis)

# Reorder 'ST-type' based on count
combined_df$`ST-type` <- factor(combined_df$`ST-type`, levels = rev(names(sort(table(combined_df$`ST-type`)))))
levels(combined_df$`ST-type`)
# Improved bar plot with count labels, color palette, and better axis handling
st_type_plot <- ggplot(combined_df, aes(x = reorder(`ST-type`, table(`ST-type`)[`ST-type`]), fill = species)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "count", position = "dodge") +  # Dodge position for separated bars by species
  geom_text(stat = "count", aes(label = ..count..), vjust = -0.3, size = 3.5, position = position_dodge(width = 0.9)) +  # Add count labels
  scale_fill_viridis(discrete = TRUE, option = "D") +  # Viridis color palette for fill
  theme_light(base_size = 15) +  # Use light theme
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, hjust = 1, size = 10),  # Rotate x-axis text for better fit
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10),  # Adjust y-axis text size
    legend.text = element_text(size = 8),  # Adjust legend text size
    legend.title = element_text(size = 10),  # Adjust legend title size
    legend.position = "bottom"  # Position legend at the bottom
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of ST-types in *Staphylococcus aureus*",  # Title with species in italics
    x = "ST-type",  # X-axis label
    y = "Count"  # Y-axis label
  )

# Save the plot with high resolution
ggsave("ST_type_distribution_enhanced.png", plot = st_type_plot, dpi = 300, width = 12, height = 7)

```

## Statistics
```{r}
# Create a contingency table of ST-type by Origin
contingency_table <- table(combined_df$`ST-type`, combined_df$Origin)

# View the contingency table
print(contingency_table)
contig_table <- as.data.frame(contingency_table)
# Load the necessary libraries
library(ggplot2)

# Assuming your data frame is already named contig_table
# Create the plot
q <- ggplot(contig_table, aes(x = Var1, y = Freq, fill = Var2)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "dodge") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Kenya" = "#1f77b4", "Tanzania" = "#ff7f0e", "Uganda" = "#2ca02c")) +
  labs(title = "Frequency of Var1 across Different Origins",
       x = "Var1",
       y = "Frequency",
       fill = "Origin") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 15) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, max(contig_table$Freq) + 10))

# Save the plot as a high-resolution image
#ggsave("frequency_plot.png", width = 12, height = 8, dpi = 300)
q
```

```{r}
# Perform chi-squared test on the contingency table
chi_test <- chisq.test(contingency_table)

# View the chi-squared test result
print(chi_test)

```
```{r}
# Perform Fisher's exact test with Monte Carlo simulation
fisher_test <- fisher.test(contingency_table, simulate.p.value = TRUE, B = 10000)

# View the Fisher's test result
print(fisher_test)

```
```{r}
# Load necessary libraries
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(broom)

# Ensure the Origin column is a factor
combined_df$Origin <- as.factor(combined_df$Origin)

# Create a contingency table for ST-type and Origin
contingency_table <- table(combined_df$`ST-type`, combined_df$Origin)

# Print the contingency table to inspect its structure
print(contingency_table)

# Check unique levels in Origin
origins <- levels(combined_df$Origin)

# Perform pairwise Fisher's Exact Test with simulation
if (length(origins) >= 2) {
  pairwise_results <- combn(origins, 2, function(x) {
    # Extract the relevant data from the contingency table
    test <- fisher.test(contingency_table[, x], alternative = "two.sided", simulate.p.value = TRUE)
    data.frame(Origin1 = x[1], Origin2 = x[2], p_value = test$p.value)
  }, simplify = FALSE)
  
  # Combine results into a data frame
  pairwise_results_df <- bind_rows(pairwise_results)
  
  # Apply Bonferroni correction
  pairwise_results_df <- pairwise_results_df %>%
    mutate(p_adjusted = p.adjust(p_value, method = "bonferroni"))
  
  # Display the pairwise results
  print(pairwise_results_df)
} else {
  print("Not enough unique origins for pairwise comparison.")
}


```
```{r}
# Load necessary libraries
library(ggplot2)
library(reshape2)
library(RColorBrewer)

# Ensure your pairwise results are in a wide format matrix for visualization
pairwise_results_matrix <- reshape2::acast(pairwise_results_df, Origin1 ~ Origin2, value.var = "p_adjusted")

# Convert the matrix to a data frame for ggplot2
melted_results <- melt(pairwise_results_matrix, na.rm = TRUE)

# Add significance levels to the results
melted_results$significance <- cut(melted_results$value, 
                                   breaks = c(-Inf, 0.001, 0.01, 0.05, Inf), 
                                   labels = c("***", "**", "*", "ns"))

# Create a heatmap with ggplot2
B <-ggplot(melted_results, aes(Var1, Var2, fill = value)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white") +
  scale_fill_gradient2(low = "blue", high = "red", mid = "white", 
                       midpoint = 0.05, limit = c(0, 1), space = "Lab", 
                       name="p-adjusted") +
  geom_text(aes(label = significance), color = "black", size = 4) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 1, 
                                   size = 12, hjust = 1)) +
  labs(x = "", y = "", title = "") +
  coord_fixed()
# Save the plot with high resolution
ggsave("pairwise_fishers_test_heatmap.png", B,dpi = 300, width = 8, height = 6)

```



```{r}
# Load required libraries
library(ggplot2)

# Melt the contingency table for ggplot
contingency_melted <- melt(contingency_table)

# Plot the heatmap without text inside the tiles and with a light background
heatmap_plot <- ggplot(contingency_melted, aes(Var1, Var2, fill = value)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white", size = 0.8) +  # Add white borders for separation
  scale_fill_distiller(palette = "Spectral", name = "Count", direction = 1) +  # Use a diverging color palette for better contrast
  labs(x = "ST-type", y = "", title = "") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +  # Minimal theme with base font size for clearer labels
  theme(
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),  # Set background to white
    plot.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),   # Ensure the plot background is white
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1, size = 12, face = "bold"),  # Bold x-axis labels for readability
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),  # Bold y-axis labels
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 16, face = "bold"),  # Center and enlarge title
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 14),  # Adjust x-axis title size
    legend.position = "right",  # Position legend on the right
    legend.text = element_text(size = 10),  # Adjust legend text size
    legend.title = element_text(size = 12)  # Adjust legend title size
  )

# Save the plot as a high-resolution PNG file with a white background
ggsave("heatmap_STtype_vs_Origin.png", plot = heatmap_plot, width = 10, height = 8, dpi = 300, bg = "white")

```

```{r}
# Load required libraries
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

# Create a summary data frame for counts of ST types by origin
summary_df <- combined_df %>%
  group_by(Origin, `ST-type`) %>%
  summarise(count = n(), .groups = 'drop')

# Filter ST-types that have a count above 10 across all origins
filtered_df <- summary_df %>%
  group_by(`ST-type`) %>%
  filter(sum(count) > 10) %>%
  ungroup()

# Create the improved bar plot
bar_plot <- ggplot(filtered_df, aes(x = `ST-type`, y = count, fill = Origin)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge(width = 0.8), width = 0.7) +  # Adjust bar and dodge width
  labs(title = "Counts of ST Types by Origin",
       x = "ST Type",
       y = "Count") +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set1") +  # Use a clean color palette
  theme_minimal(base_size = 15) +  # Increase base font size for readability
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),  # Rotate x-axis labels
        legend.title = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "grey90"),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),  # Remove minor gridlines
        plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 16),  # Bold and increase title size
        legend.position = "top") +  # Move legend to the top for better balance
  geom_text(aes(label = count), position = position_dodge(width = 0.8), vjust = -0.5, size = 4)  # Add counts above bars

# Save the improved plot without significance annotations

ggsave("ST_type_distribution_filtered_barplot.png", plot = bar_plot, dpi = 300, width = 12, height = 7)

```

```{r}

library(patchwork)

# Combine the heatmap_plot and B with a title
combined_plot <- (heatmap_plot + B) + 
  plot_layout(widths = c(3, 1)) + 
  plot_annotation(title = "ST-types distribution in East Africa", 
                  theme = theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 16, face = "bold")))

# Display the combined plot
print(combined_plot)


ggsave("combined_plot.png", plot = combined_plot, dpi = 300, width = 12, height = 7)

```

```{r}
# Set working directory
setwd("D:/mrsa/spa_results/")  # Adjust if needed

# List all CSV files
csv_files <- list.files(pattern = "*.csv$", full.names = TRUE)

# Function to extract the spa type (last word of the second row)
extract_spa_type <- function(file) {
  lines <- readLines(file)  # Read file as raw lines
  
  if (length(lines) >= 3) {  # Ensure at least 2 lines exist
    last_column <- unlist(strsplit(lines[3], "\\s+"))  # Split second line by spaces/tabs
    spa_type <- tail(last_column, 1)  # Get last element (spa type)
    return(data.frame(file_name = basename(file), spa_type = spa_type, stringsAsFactors = FALSE))
  } else {
    return(NULL)  # Skip if the file has insufficient lines
  }
}

# Apply function to all files and combine results
spa_types_df <- do.call(rbind, lapply(csv_files, extract_spa_type))

# View final dataframe
print(spa_types_df)

# Remove rows where 'spa_type' does not start with "t"
spa_types_df <- spa_types_df[grepl("^t", spa_types_df$spa_type), ]

# View cleaned dataframe
print(spa_types_df)


```

```{r}
# Load necessary package
library(dplyr)

# Rename 'file_name' column to 'sample_id' and clean sample IDs
spa_types_df <- spa_types_df %>%
  rename(sample_id = file_name) %>%
  mutate(sample_id = gsub("_spa_typing\\.csv$", "", sample_id))

# Define genome ID ranges for each country
uganda_ids <- paste0("ERR", 4692235:4692276)
kenya_ids <- c(
  paste0("ERR", 3150908:3151001), "ERR3218227",
  paste0("ERR", 1764887:1765052),
  paste0("ERR", 1791869:1791882),
  paste0("ERR", 2436451:2436455))
tanzania_ids <- c(
  paste0("ERR", 12916291:12916300),
  paste0("ERR", 12511686:12511850))

# Assign country based on sample_id
spa_types_df <- spa_types_df %>%
  mutate(country = case_when(
    sample_id %in% uganda_ids ~ "Uganda",
    sample_id %in% kenya_ids  ~ "Kenya",
    sample_id %in% tanzania_ids ~ "Tanzania",
    TRUE ~ "Unknown" # Assign 'Unknown' if sample ID is not in the lists
  ))

# View cleaned dataframe
print(spa_types_df)

```

```{r}
# Load libraries
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(scales)

# Ensure spa_type is a factor
spa_types_df$spa_type <- as.factor(spa_types_df$spa_type)

# Frequency table and sort
spa_freq <- table(spa_types_df$spa_type)
spa_freq_sorted <- sort(spa_freq, decreasing = TRUE)

# Top 15 spa types
top_n <- 15
top_spa <- names(spa_freq_sorted)[1:top_n]

# Create summary table with counts and percentages
top_spa_df <- spa_types_df %>%
  filter(spa_type %in% top_spa) %>%
  count(spa_type, name = "count") %>%
  mutate(percentage = 100 * count / sum(count)) %>%
  arrange(desc(percentage))

# View summary table
print(top_spa_df)

# Create the plot with percentages
p <- ggplot(top_spa_df, aes(x = reorder(spa_type, -percentage), y = percentage)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "#2c7fb8") +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste0(round(percentage, 1), "%")), 
            vjust = -0.5, size = 4.2) +
  labs(
    title = "Top 15 Most Common spa Types",
    x = "spa Type",
    y = "Percentage (%)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5)
  )
p
# Save high-resolution plot (300 dpi)
ggsave(
  filename = "D:/mrsa/spa_r/top15_spa_types_percentage.jpg",
  plot = p,
  width = 10,
  height = 6,
  dpi = 600
)
# Save the top spa types table as CSV
write.csv(
  top_spa_df,
  file = "D:/mrsa/spa_r/top15_spa_types_summary.csv",
  row.names = FALSE
)

```


```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(scales)
library(viridis)



# Set number of top spa types per country
top_per_country <- 10

# Get top N spa types per country
top_spa_country <- spa_types_df %>%
  group_by(country) %>%
  count(spa_type) %>%
  slice_max(order_by = n, n = top_per_country) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  distinct(spa_type) %>%
  pull(spa_type)

# Prepare data for percentage plot
spa_filtered <- spa_types_df %>%
  filter(spa_type %in% top_spa_country) %>%
  group_by(country, spa_type) %>%
  summarise(count = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  group_by(country) %>%
  mutate(percentage = count / sum(count) * 100) %>%
  ungroup()

# Plot as percent bar chart, facetted by country
p_country <- ggplot(spa_filtered, aes(x = reorder(spa_type, -percentage), y = percentage, fill = spa_type)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", show.legend = FALSE) +
  facet_wrap(~ country, scales = "free_x") +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = percent_format(scale = 1)) +
  scale_fill_viridis_d(option = "plasma", direction = -1) +
  labs(
    x = "spa Type",
    y = "Percentage (%)",
    title = "Top spa Types by Country "
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 10),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 11),
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold")
  )

# Print plot
print(p_country)

# Save high-res image
ggsave("D:/mrsa/spa_r/top_spa_types_by_country.jpg", p_country, width = 12, height = 8, dpi = 600)

```

```{r}
# Create contingency table
contingency_table <- table(spa_types_df$spa_type, spa_types_df$country)

# Filter to only include spa types with sufficient counts (e.g., at least 5 observations)
filtered_table <- contingency_table[rowSums(contingency_table) >= 10, ]

# Perform chi-square test
chi_test <- chisq.test(filtered_table)
chi_test
```

```{r}
# Pairwise chi-square tests
countries <- unique(spa_types_df$country)
country_pairs <- combn(countries, 2, simplify = FALSE)

pairwise_results <- lapply(country_pairs, function(pair) {
  pair_data <- spa_types_df %>% filter(country %in% pair)
  pair_table <- table(pair_data$spa_type, pair_data$country)
  pair_table <- pair_table[rowSums(pair_table) >= 5, ] # Filter rare types
  if(nrow(pair_table) > 0) {  # Added closing parenthesis and > 0 check
    test <- chisq.test(pair_table)
    data.frame(country1 = pair[1], country2 = pair[2], 
               p.value = test$p.value)
  }
})

pairwise_df <- do.call(rbind, pairwise_results)
pairwise_df$p.adj <- p.adjust(pairwise_df$p.value, method = "BH")
pairwise_df
```

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(ggsignif)
library(rcompanion)

# 1. Create contingency table and filter rare spa types
full_table <- table(spa_types_df$spa_type, spa_types_df$country)
filtered_table <- full_table[rowSums(full_table) >= 5, ]  # Keep common types

# 2. Fisher's exact test (simulate p-value for sparse data)
fisher_test <- fisher.test(filtered_table, simulate.p.value = TRUE)

# 3. Define all pairwise country combinations
country_pairs <- combn(unique(spa_types_df$country), 2, simplify = FALSE)

# 4. Calculate Cramer's V safely per country pair
effect_sizes <- lapply(country_pairs, function(pair) {
  pair_table <- filtered_table[, pair]
  pair_table <- pair_table[rowSums(pair_table) > 0, ]  # remove zero rows
  
  if (nrow(pair_table) >= 2) {
    cramerV(pair_table, ci = FALSE)
  } else {
    NA
  }
})

# Optional: name effect sizes for reference
names(effect_sizes) <- sapply(country_pairs, function(x) paste(x, collapse = "_vs_"))
effect_sizes_df <- data.frame(
  Country_Pair = names(effect_sizes),
  Cramers_V = unlist(effect_sizes)
)

# 5. Prepare data for plotting proportions
plot_data <- spa_types_df %>%
  count(country, spa_type) %>%
  group_by(country) %>%
  mutate(prop = n / sum(n)) %>%
  filter(spa_type %in% rownames(filtered_table))

# 6. Plot: Stacked bar of spa types by country with statistics
p <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = country, y = prop, fill = spa_type)) +
  geom_col(position = "fill") +
  
  # Add annotation for p-value
  annotate(
    "text", x = 1.5, y = 1.07, 
    label = paste("Fisher's p =", signif(fisher_test$p.value, 2)),
    size = 5
  ) +
  
  # Add annotation for mean Cramer's V
  annotate(
    "text", x = 1.5, y = 1.13, 
    label = paste("Mean Cramer's V =", signif(mean(unlist(effect_sizes), na.rm = TRUE), 2)),
    size = 5
  ) +
  
  # Final cosmetics
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent, expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.1))) +
  labs(
    title = "Spa Type Distribution by Country",
    x = "", 
    y = "Proportion", 
    fill = "Spa Type"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    legend.position = "right",
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)
  )

# Print plot
print(p)



ggsave("D:/mrsa/spa_r/spa_type_by_country_distribution.jpg", plot = p, dpi = 600, width = 10, height = 6)

write.csv(effect_sizes_df, "D:/mrsa/spa_r/cramers_v_by_country_pair.csv", row.names = FALSE)


```

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(viridis)

# Prepare heatmap data
heatmap_data <- spa_types_df %>%
  group_by(country, spa_type) %>%
  summarise(count = n(), .groups = 'drop') %>%
  group_by(country) %>%
  mutate(prop = count / sum(count)) %>%
  group_by(spa_type) %>%
  filter(n_distinct(country) >= 2 & sum(count) >= 5)

# Create the heatmap without labels
p_heatmap <- ggplot(heatmap_data, aes(x = country, y = spa_type, fill = prop)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 0.4) +
  scale_fill_viridis(
    name = "Proportion",
    option = "D",
    direction = -1,
    limits = c(0, max(heatmap_data$prop)),
    labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1)
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Spa Type Distribution Across Countries",
    subtitle = "Spa types present in â‰Ą2 countries with â‰Ą5 total isolates",
    x = "Country",
    y = "Spa Type"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    legend.position = "right"
  )

# Display the heatmap
print(p_heatmap)

# Save the heatmap as high-resolution image
ggsave("D:/mrsa/spa_r/spa_type_heatmap.jpg", plot = p_heatmap, width = 10, height = 8, dpi = 600)


```

```{r}
# First identify significantly different spa types (example using t1476)
sig_type <- "t1476"
sig_data <- spa_types_df %>%
  filter(spa_type == sig_type) %>%
  group_by(country) %>%
  summarise(count = n(), total = nrow(spa_types_df[spa_types_df$country == first(country),]))

# Perform pairwise tests for this type
pairwise_results <- pairwise.prop.test(
  x = sig_data$count,
  n = sig_data$total,
  p.adjust.method = "BH"
)

# Create plot with significance bars
ggplot(sig_data, aes(x = country, y = count/total, fill = country)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  geom_signif(comparisons = list(c("Tanzania", "Kenya")), 
              annotations = format(pairwise_results$p.value[1,2], digits = 2),
              y_position = 0.9*max(sig_data$count/sig_data$total)) +
  labs(title = paste("Prevalence of", sig_type, "by Country"),
       y = "Proportion") +
  theme_minimal()
```

mec profiling
```{r}
setwd("D:/mrsa/abricate/AMR/")
# List all summary.txt files in the directory
file_list <- list.files(pattern = "summary\\.txt$", full.names = TRUE)

# Initialize an empty list to store data frames
df_list <- list()

# Define the expected column names
common_cols <- c("FILE", "SEQUENCE", "START", "END", "STRAND", "GENE", 
                 "COVERAGE", "COVERAGE_MAP", "GAPS", "%COVERAGE", 
                 "%IDENTITY", "DATABASE", "ACCESSION", "PRODUCT", "RESISTANCE")

# Loop through each file and read it into a data frame
for (file in file_list) {
  # Check if the file is not empty
  if (file.info(file)$size > 0) {
    # Try to read the file and skip if there's an error
    try({
      df <- read.table(file, header = TRUE, sep = "\t", fill = TRUE, comment.char = "", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
      
      # Ensure that we have at least the expected number of columns (to avoid NAs)
      if (ncol(df) >= length(common_cols)) {
        # Subset the dataframe to keep only the expected columns, or add missing ones
        colnames(df) <- common_cols[1:ncol(df)]
        missing_cols <- setdiff(common_cols, colnames(df))
        
        # Add missing columns with NA values if necessary
        if (length(missing_cols) > 0) {
          df[missing_cols] <- NA
        }
        
        # Reorder columns to match the common structure
        df <- df[, common_cols]
        
        # Store each valid data frame in the list
        df_list[[file]] <- df
      } else {
        cat("Skipping file due to insufficient columns:", file, "\n")
      }
    }, silent = TRUE)
  } else {
    cat("Skipping empty file:", file, "\n")
  }
}

# Combine all valid data frames into one
final_df <- do.call(rbind, df_list)



# Print the combined data frame to check
print(final_df)

# Extract genome IDs from the FILE column
final_df$GENOME_ID <- gsub(".*(ERR[0-9]+).*", "\\1", final_df$FILE)

# Define genome ID ranges for each country
uganda_ids <- paste0("ERR", 4692235:4692276)
kenya_ids <- c(
  paste0("ERR", 3150908:3151001), "ERR3218227",
  paste0("ERR", 1764887:1765052),
  paste0("ERR", 1791869:1791882),
  paste0("ERR", 2436451:2436455))
tanzania_ids <- c(
  paste0("ERR", 12916291:12916300),
  paste0("ERR", 12511686:12511850))

# Create a mapping dataframe
country_mapping <- data.frame(
  GENOME_ID = c(uganda_ids, kenya_ids, tanzania_ids),
  COUNTRY = c(
    rep("Uganda", length(uganda_ids)),
    rep("Kenya", length(kenya_ids)),
    rep("Tanzania", length(tanzania_ids))
  )
)

# Merge country information into final_df
final_df <- merge(final_df, country_mapping, by = "GENOME_ID", all.x = TRUE)

# View the updated dataframe
head(final_df)



# Load dplyr package
library(dplyr)

# Filter the dataframe and select relevant columns
filtered_df <- final_df %>%
  filter(grepl("mec", GENE, ignore.case = TRUE)) %>%
  select(GENOME_ID, GENE, RESISTANCE, COUNTRY)

# View the filtered dataframe
head(filtered_df)





```

```{r}
country_counts <- table(filtered_df$COUNTRY)
total_count <- sum(country_counts)  # Total number of genome entries
country_percentage <- (country_counts / total_count) * 100
country_summary <- data.frame(
  Country = names(country_counts),
  Count = as.integer(country_counts),
  Percentage = round(country_percentage, 2)
)

print(country_summary)



```

```{r}
# Set working directory
setwd("D:/mrsa/relaxed_sccmec_results")

# List only the .tsv files that match the pattern: ERR followed by digits and ending in .tsv
target_files <- list.files(pattern = "^ERR[0-9]+\\.tsv$")

# Read them into a named list of data frames
tsv_list <- lapply(setNames(target_files, target_files), read.delim)

# View the names of the loaded data frames
names(tsv_list)# Set working directory
setwd("D:/mrsa/relaxed_sccmec_results")

# List only the target .tsv files matching the pattern
target_files <- list.files(pattern = "^ERR[0-9]+\\.tsv$")

# Read all matching .tsv files and combine them into one data frame
combined_df <- do.call(rbind, lapply(target_files, function(file) {
  df <- read.delim(file)
  df$file_name <- file  # Optionally add the file name as a column for reference
  return(df)
}))

# View the combined data frame
head(combined_df)


```

```{r}
# Define sample IDs for each country
uganda_ids <- paste0("ERR", 4692235:4692276)

kenya_ids <- c(
  paste0("ERR", 3150908:3151001), "ERR3218227",
  paste0("ERR", 1764887:1765052),
  paste0("ERR", 1791869:1791882),
  paste0("ERR", 2436451:2436455)
)

tanzania_ids <- c(
  paste0("ERR", 12916291:12916300),
  paste0("ERR", 12511686:12511850)
)

# Assign COUNTRY based on sample membership
combined_df$Country <- ifelse(combined_df$sample %in% uganda_ids, "Uganda",
                        ifelse(combined_df$sample %in% kenya_ids, "Kenya",
                        ifelse(combined_df$sample %in% tanzania_ids, "Tanzania", "Unknown")))

# Check results
table(combined_df$Country)
head(combined_df)

```

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(writexl)

combined_df <- combined_df %>%
  select(sample, type, subtype, targets, mecA, Country)
combined_df[combined_df == "-"] <- "none"
write_xlsx(combined_df, "D:/mrsa/relaxed_sccmec_results/selected_sccmec_results.xlsx")

```


```{r}
library(tidyverse)

# Clean subtype labels and create binary mecA column
combined_df <- combined_df %>%
  mutate(
    subtype = ifelse(subtype %in% c("none", "multiple"), NA, subtype),
    mecA_present = ifelse(mecA == "+", "Present", "Absent")
  ) %>%
  filter(type != "none")  # Remove untyped samples if needed
```


```{r}
chisq_test <- chisq.test(table(combined_df$type, combined_df$Country))
chisq_test  # Check p-value
```
```{r}
pairwise_fisher <- function(data, country1, country2) {
  tab <- table(data$type[data$Country %in% c(country1, country2)], 
               data$Country[data$Country %in% c(country1, country2)])
  fisher.test(tab, simulate.p.value = TRUE)
}

# Example: Kenya vs. Tanzania
pairwise_fisher(combined_df, "Kenya", "Tanzania")
```
