```{r}
pairwise_fisher(combined_df, "Kenya", "Uganda")
pairwise_fisher(combined_df, "Uganda", "Tanzania")

```

```{r}
# Ensure the reference level is "Absent"
combined_df$mecA_present <- factor(combined_df$mecA_present, 
                                   levels = c("Absent", "Present"))

# Now run glm
mecA_model <- glm(mecA_present ~ Country, 
                  data = combined_df, 
                  family = "binomial")
summary(mecA_model)
```

```{r}
library(ggplot2)

# Create frequency table
type_country <- as.data.frame(table(combined_df$type, combined_df$Country))
colnames(type_country) <- c("Type", "Country", "Count")

# Create the plot
sccmec_plot <- ggplot(type_country, aes(x = Country, y = Count, fill = Type)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "stack") +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set3") +  # Optional: prettier color palette
  labs(title = "Distribution of SCCmec Types by Country",
       x = "Country", y = "Isolate Count", fill = "SCCmec Type") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +  # Bigger text for publication
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
    legend.position = "right"
  )

# Print the plot
print(sccmec_plot)

# Save as high-resolution TIFF
ggsave("sccmec/SCCmec_Distribution_by_Country.jpg", 
       plot = sccmec_plot,
       width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 600)

```

```{r}
# Open a high-resolution JPEG device
jpeg("sccmec/SCCmec_Mosaic_Distribution_by_Country.jpg", 
     width = 10, height = 6, units = "in", res = 600, quality = 100)

# Create the mosaic plot
mosaicplot(table(combined_df$type, combined_df$Country),
           main = "SCCmec Type Distribution by Country",
           xlab = "SCCmec Type", ylab = "Country",
           color = TRUE, shade = TRUE)

# Close the graphics device
dev.off()

```






```{r}
# Create a matrix of p-values from pairwise tests
pairwise_results <- matrix(NA, nrow = 3, ncol = 3)
rownames(pairwise_results) <- colnames(pairwise_results) <- c("Kenya", "Tanzania", "Uganda")

# Fill with your p-values
pairwise_results["Kenya", "Tanzania"] <- 0.0004998
pairwise_results["Kenya", "Uganda"] <- 0.02349
pairwise_results["Tanzania", "Uganda"] <- 0.001499

# Plot as a heatmap
library(reshape2)
melted_results <- melt(pairwise_results, na.rm = TRUE)

p <- ggplot(melted_results, aes(Var1, Var2, fill = value)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white") +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "red", high = "blue", 
                     name = "p-value", 
                     limits = c(0, 0.03)) +
  geom_text(aes(label = format.pval(value, digits = 2)), 
            color = "white", size = 4) +
  labs(title = "Pairwise Fisher Exact Test Results",
       x = "", y = "") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
ggsave("sccmec/fishers_results.jpg", p, width = 10, height = 8, dpi = 600)

```

```{r}
table(combined_df$Country, combined_df$mecA_present)
```






```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(scales)

p <-ggplot(combined_df, aes(x = Country, fill = type)) +
  geom_bar(position = "fill", width = 0.8, color = "white", linewidth = 0.1) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    name = "SCCmec Type",
    values = c("III" = "#E69F00", "IV" = "#56B4E9", "V" = "#009E73", "VI" = "#F0E442"),
    na.value = "grey90"
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = percent_format(), 
                    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.05))) +
  labs(
    x = NULL,
    y = "Proportion of isolates",
    title = "Distribution of SCCmec types by country"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "top",
    legend.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 10),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 9),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5, size = 14),
    axis.title.y = element_text(margin = margin(r = 10)),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, vjust = 1),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    plot.caption = element_text(color = "grey50", size = 8),
    plot.margin = unit(c(1, 1, 1, 1), "cm")
  ) +
  guides(fill = guide_legend(nrow = 1, byrow = TRUE))
p
ggsave("sccmec/SCCmec_types.jpg",
       plot = p,
       dpi = 600,                     # High resolution
       width = 8,                     # Inches (adjust as needed)
       height = 6)            # Lossless compression
```



```{r}
library(sf)
library(rnaturalearth)
library(tidyverse)
library(scatterpie)  # Key package for pie charts on maps
library(ggrepel)     # For better label placement

# Install packages if needed
if (!require("scatterpie")) install.packages("scatterpie")
if (!require("ggrepel")) install.packages("ggrepel")

# 1. Prepare your data - count SCCmec types by country
type_counts <- combined_df %>%
  count(Country, type) %>%
  group_by(Country) %>%
  mutate(percent = n/sum(n)*100) %>%
  ungroup()

# Prepare pie chart data with coordinates
pie_data <- type_counts %>%
  mutate(
    lon = case_when(
      Country == "Kenya" ~ 37.8,
      Country == "Tanzania" ~ 34.9,
      Country == "Uganda" ~ 32.4
    ),
    lat = case_when(
      Country == "Kenya" ~ -0.5,
      Country == "Tanzania" ~ -6.4,
      Country == "Uganda" ~ 1.4
    ),
    # Calculate radius proportional to sample count
    radius = sqrt(n) * 0.2
  ) %>%
  select(-percent) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = type, values_from = n, values_fill = 0)

pie_data <- tibble::tibble(
  Country = c(
    "Kenya", "Kenya", "Kenya",
    "Tanzania", "Tanzania",
    "Uganda", "Uganda", "Uganda"
  ),
  lon = c(
    37.8, 37.8, 37.8,
    34.9, 34.9,
    32.4, 32.4, 32.4
  ),
  lat = c(
    -0.5, -0.5, -0.5,
    -6.4, -6.4,
    1.4, 1.4, 1.4
  ),
  radius = c(
    sqrt(5)*0.2,  # Kenya - III
    sqrt(7)*0.2,  # Kenya - IV
    sqrt(7)*0.2,  # Kenya - V
    sqrt(1)*0.2,  # Tanzania - IV
    sqrt(73)*0.2, # Tanzania - V
    sqrt(1)*0.2,  # Uganda - III
    sqrt(5)*0.2,  # Uganda - V
    sqrt(2)*0.2   # Uganda - VI
  ),
  III = c(5, 0, 0, 0, 0, 1, 0, 0),
  IV  = c(0, 7, 0, 1, 0, 0, 0, 0),
  V   = c(0, 0, 7, 0, 73, 0, 5, 0),
  VI  = c(0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 2)
)




# Get country shapes
east_africa <- ne_countries(scale = 50, returnclass = "sf") %>%
  filter(name %in% c("Kenya", "Tanzania", "Uganda"))

# Create the visualization
ggplot() +
  # Base map
  geom_sf(data = east_africa, fill = "gray95", color = "white") +
  
  # Add pie charts
  geom_scatterpie(
    data = pie_data,
    aes(x = lon, y = lat, r = radius),
    cols = c("III", "IV", "V", "VI"),
    color = NA,
    alpha = 0.8
  ) +
  
  # Custom color scale
  scale_fill_manual(
    name = "SCCmec Type",
    values = c("III" = "#E69F00", "IV" = "#56B4E9", "V" = "#009E73", "VI" = "#F0E442"),
    breaks = c("III", "IV", "V", "VI")
  ) +
  
  # Add country labels with sample counts
  geom_label_repel(
    data = pie_data,
    aes(x = lon, y = lat, 
        label = paste0(Country, "\nTotal: ", III+IV+V+VI)),
    box.padding = 1,
    point.padding = 0.5,
    min.segment.length = 0
  ) +
  
  # Titles and theme
  labs(
    title = "SCCmec Type Distribution in East Africa",
    subtitle = "Pie size represents sample count, colors show type distribution",
  ) +
  theme_void() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),
    legend.position = "right"
  )
```


```{r}
library(sf)
library(rnaturalearth)
library(tidyverse)
library(scatterpie)
library(ggrepel)

# Consolidated pie data - One row per country
pie_data <- tibble::tibble(
  Country = c("Kenya", "Tanzania", "Uganda"),
  lon = c(37.8, 34.9, 32.4),
  lat = c(-0.5, -6.4, 1.4),
  III = c(5, 0, 1),
  IV  = c(7, 1, 0),
  V   = c(7, 73, 5),
  VI  = c(0, 0, 2)
) %>%
  mutate(
    total = III + IV + V + VI,
    radius = sqrt(total) * 0.2
  )

# Get country shapes
east_africa <- ne_countries(scale = 50, returnclass = "sf") %>%
  filter(name %in% c("Kenya", "Tanzania", "Uganda"))

# Plot
p <- ggplot() +
  # Base map
  geom_sf(data = east_africa, fill = "gray95", color = "gray60", size = 0.4) +
  
  # Pie charts
  geom_scatterpie(
    data = pie_data,
    aes(x = lon, y = lat, r = radius),
    cols = c("III", "IV", "V", "VI"),
    color = "white",
    alpha = 0.9
  ) +
  
  # Add labels with total counts
  geom_label_repel(
    data = pie_data,
    aes(x = lon, y = lat, label = paste0(Country, "\nTotal: ", total)),
    size = 4,
    fontface = "bold",
    fill = "white",
    box.padding = 0.5,
    point.padding = 0.5,
    segment.color = "gray50"
  ) +

  # Custom color palette
  scale_fill_manual(
    name = "SCCmec Type",
    values = c("III" = "#E69F00", "IV" = "#56B4E9", "V" = "#009E73", "VI" = "#F0E442")
  ) +
  
  # Titles
  labs(
    title = "SCCmec Type Distribution in East Africa",
    subtitle = "Pie size proportional to sample count; Colors indicate SCCmec types"
  ) +

  # Theme
  coord_sf(xlim = c(30, 41), ylim = c(-12, 5), expand = FALSE) +
  theme_void(base_family = "Helvetica") +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 16, face = "bold"),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 12),
    legend.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    legend.position = "right"
  )

# Print the plot
print(p)

# Save high-resolution version
ggsave("sccmec/SCCmec_EastAfrica_Distribution.jpg", p, width = 10, height = 8, dpi = 600)

```






```{r}
library(igraph)
library(ggraph)

# Create adjacency matrix from targets
edges <- combined_df$targets %>%
  str_split(",") %>%
  map(~combn(., 2, simplify = FALSE)) %>%
  unlist() %>%
  matrix(ncol = 2, byrow = TRUE) %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  count(V1, V2)

graph <- graph_from_data_frame(edges, directed = FALSE)

ggraph(graph, layout = "fr") +
  geom_edge_link(aes(width = n), alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_node_point(size = 8, color = "tomato") +
  geom_node_text(aes(label = name), repel = TRUE) +
  theme_void()
```

```{r}
library(igraph)
library(ggraph)
library(tidyverse)

# 1. Preprocess the data more carefully
edges <- combined_df %>%
  separate_rows(targets, sep = ",") %>%
  mutate(targets = str_trim(targets)) %>%
  group_by(sample) %>%
  filter(n() > 1) %>%  # Only samples with multiple targets
  do({
    targets <- unique(.$targets)
    if(length(targets) >= 2) {
      expand_grid(V1 = targets, V2 = targets) %>%
        filter(V1 < V2)  # Avoid duplicate pairs
    } else {
      NULL
    }
  }) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  count(V1, V2, name = "weight")

# 2. Create graph with meaningful attributes
graph <- graph_from_data_frame(edges, directed = FALSE) %>%
  set_edge_attr("weight", value = edges$weight) %>%
  simplify(edge.attr.comb = "sum")  # Combine multiple edges

# Calculate node importance
V(graph)$degree <- degree(graph)
V(graph)$betweenness <- betweenness(graph)

# 3. Enhanced visualization
set.seed(123)  # For reproducibility
ggraph(graph, layout = "fr") +
  # Edges with weight-based appearance
  geom_edge_link(
    aes(width = weight, alpha = weight),
    color = "gray50",
    show.legend = TRUE
  ) +
  scale_edge_width(range = c(0.5, 3)) +
  scale_edge_alpha(range = c(0.3, 0.8)) +
  
  # Nodes with importance-based sizing
  geom_node_point(
    aes(size = degree, fill = betweenness),
    shape = 21,
    color = "white",
    stroke = 0.5
  ) +
  scale_size_continuous(range = c(4, 15), name = "Degree") +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "magma", name = "Betweenness") +
  
  # Node labels with smart positioning
  geom_node_text(
    aes(label = name, filter = degree > quantile(degree, 0.5)),
    repel = TRUE,
    size = 4,
    point.padding = 0.2,
    box.padding = 0.3,
    color = "black"
  ) +
  
  # Layout and theme
  theme_graph(base_family = "sans") +
  labs(
    title = "Co-occurrence Network of SCCmec Elements",
    subtitle = "Edge width shows frequency of co-occurrence\nNode size indicates degree, color shows betweenness centrality",
    caption = paste("Total samples:", nrow(combined_df))
  ) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5),
    legend.position = "bottom"
  )
```

```{r}
library(igraph)
library(ggraph)
library(tidyverse)

# 1. Preprocess data - Build edges from co-occurring SCCmec elements in samples
edges <- combined_df %>%
  separate_rows(targets, sep = ",") %>%
  mutate(targets = str_trim(targets)) %>%
  group_by(sample) %>%
  filter(n() > 1) %>%
  do({
    targets <- unique(.$targets)
    if(length(targets) >= 2) {
      expand_grid(V1 = targets, V2 = targets) %>%
        filter(V1 < V2)
    } else NULL
  }) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  count(V1, V2, name = "weight")

# 2. Build graph object
graph <- graph_from_data_frame(edges, directed = FALSE) %>%
  set_edge_attr("weight", value = edges$weight) %>%
  simplify(edge.attr.comb = "sum")

# 3. Calculate centrality metrics
V(graph)$degree <- degree(graph)
V(graph)$betweenness <- betweenness(graph)

# 4. Enhanced visualization
set.seed(123)
ggraph(graph, layout = "fr") +
  # Edges based on co-occurrence strength
  geom_edge_link(
    aes(width = weight, alpha = weight),
    color = "gray60"
  ) +
  scale_edge_width(range = c(0.3, 3), guide = "none") +
  scale_edge_alpha(range = c(0.2, 0.8), guide = "none") +

  # Nodes with size = degree, fill = betweenness centrality
  geom_node_point(
    aes(size = degree, fill = betweenness),
    shape = 21, color = "black", stroke = 0.3
  ) +
  scale_size_continuous(range = c(3, 12), name = "Degree (Connections)") +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "plasma", name = "Betweenness Centrality") +

  # Label more important nodes
  geom_node_text(
    aes(label = name),
    size = 4,
    fontface = "bold",
    repel = TRUE,
    color = "black"
  ) +

  # Final plot theme
  theme_graph(base_family = "Helvetica") +
  labs(
    title = "SCCmec Element Co-occurrence Network",
    subtitle = "Edge width: Co-occurrence frequency | Node size: Degree | Fill: Betweenness centrality",
    caption = glue::glue("Total samples analyzed: {nrow(combined_df)}")
  ) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 16, face = "bold"),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 12),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.box = "vertical"
  )

```


```{r}
library(igraph)
library(ggraph)
library(tidyverse)

# Preprocess data
edges <- combined_df %>%
  separate_rows(targets, sep = ",") %>%
  mutate(targets = str_trim(targets)) %>%
  group_by(sample) %>%
  filter(n() > 1) %>%
  do({
    targets <- unique(.$targets)
    if(length(targets) >= 2) {
      expand_grid(V1 = targets, V2 = targets) %>%
        filter(V1 < V2)
    } else NULL
  }) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  count(V1, V2, name = "weight")

# Build graph
graph <- graph_from_data_frame(edges, directed = FALSE) %>%
  set_edge_attr("weight", value = edges$weight) %>%
  simplify(edge.attr.comb = "sum")

# Centrality
V(graph)$degree <- degree(graph)
V(graph)$betweenness <- betweenness(graph)

# Create the plot and assign to object
library(glue)  # Required for glue::glue()

set.seed(123)
network_plot <- ggraph(graph, layout = "fr") +
  geom_edge_link(
    aes(width = weight, alpha = weight),
    color = "gray60"
  ) +
  scale_edge_width(range = c(0.3, 3), guide = "none") +
  scale_edge_alpha(range = c(0.2, 0.8), guide = "none") +
  geom_node_point(
    aes(size = degree, fill = betweenness),
    shape = 21, color = "black", stroke = 0.3
  ) +
  scale_size_continuous(range = c(3, 12), name = "Degree (Connections)") +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "plasma", name = "Betweenness Centrality") +
  geom_node_text(
    aes(label = name),
    size = 4,
    fontface = "bold",
    repel = TRUE,
    color = "black"
  ) +
  theme_graph(base_family = "Helvetica") +
  labs(
    title = "SCCmec Element Co-occurrence Network",
    subtitle = "Edge width: Co-occurrence frequency | Node size: Degree | Fill: Betweenness centrality",
    caption = glue::glue("Total samples analyzed: {nrow(combined_df)}")
  ) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 16, face = "bold"),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 12),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.box = "vertical"
  )

ggsave("sccmec/sccmec_network_plot.jpg", plot = network_plot, width = 10, height = 8, dpi = 600)

```

#Prophages
```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(fs)

base_dir <- "D:/mrsa/phigaro_results"

# Get list of all phigaro directories
phigaro_dirs <- dir_ls(base_dir, type = "directory", regexp = "_phigaro$")

# Function to read with consistent column types
read_phigaro_tsv <- function(dir_path) {
  sample_id <- str_remove(basename(dir_path), "_phigaro$")
  tsv_path <- file.path(dir_path, "scaffolds.phigaro.tsv")
  
  if (file.exists(tsv_path) && file.size(tsv_path) > 0) {
    read_tsv(
      tsv_path,
      col_types = cols(
        scaffold = col_character(),
        id = col_character(),
        begin = col_double(),
        end = col_double(),
        transposable = col_logical(),
        taxonomy = col_character()
      )
    ) %>% 
      mutate(sample_id = sample_id, .before = 1)
  }
}

# Read and combine all files
combined_data <- map_dfr(phigaro_dirs, read_phigaro_tsv)

# View results
glimpse(combined_data)
```

```{r}
library(dplyr)

uganda_ids <- paste0("ERR", 4692235:4692276)

kenya_ids <- c(
  paste0("ERR", 3150908:3151001), "ERR3218227",
  paste0("ERR", 1764887:1765052),
  paste0("ERR", 1791869:1791882),
  paste0("ERR", 2436451:2436455)
)

tanzania_ids <- c(
  paste0("ERR", 12916291:12916300),
  paste0("ERR", 12511686:12511850)
)                                             

# Add Country column
combined_data <- combined_data %>%
  mutate(
    Country = case_when(
      sample_id %in% uganda_ids    ~ "Uganda",
      sample_id %in% kenya_ids     ~ "Kenya",
      sample_id %in% tanzania_ids ~ "Tanzania",
      TRUE                         ~ "Unknown"
    )
  )

```


```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

# Calculate prophage length
combined_data <- combined_data %>%
  mutate(prophage_length = end - begin)

# Histogram Plot
prophage_length_plot <- ggplot(combined_data, aes(x = prophage_length)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 1000, fill = "#984EA3", color = "black", alpha = 0.8) +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::comma, 
                     breaks = seq(0, max(combined_data$prophage_length), by = 10000)) +
  labs(title = "Distribution of Prophage Lengths",
       x = "Prophage Length (bp)",
       y = "Number of Prophages") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    axis.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 12),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_line(color = "gray80"),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )

# Display the plot
print(prophage_length_plot)

# Save high-resolution image
ggsave("prophage_length_distribution.png",
       plot = prophage_length_plot,
       width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 600)

```


```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(viridis)

# Most common scaffolds
scaffold_summary <- combined_data %>%
  count(scaffold, sort = TRUE) %>%
  slice_max(n, n = 10)

# Bar plot
scaffold_plot <- ggplot(scaffold_summary, aes(x = reorder(scaffold, n), y = n)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = viridis::viridis(10)[7], color = "black") +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(title = "Top 10 Scaffolds with Most Prophages", 
       x = "Scaffold", 
       y = "Number of Prophages") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    axis.text = element_text(size = 12),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_line(color = "gray80")
  )

# Display the plot
print(scaffold_plot)

# Save high-resolution image
ggsave("top10_scaffolds_prophages.png",
       plot = scaffold_plot,
       width = 8, height = 5, dpi = 600)

```

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(viridis)

# Enhanced boxplot
tax_length_plot <- ggplot(combined_data, aes(x = taxonomy, y = prophage_length, fill = taxonomy)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = 21, outlier.size = 1.5, color = "black") +
  scale_fill_viridis(discrete = TRUE, option = "D") +
  scale_y_log10() +  # log scale to normalize skewed lengths
  labs(
    title = "Prophage Lengths by Taxonomic Group", 
    y = "Length (bp, log10 scale)", 
    x = "Taxonomy"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 12),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
    legend.position = "none"
  )

# Display the plot
print(tax_length_plot)

# Save high-resolution image
ggsave("prophage_lengths_by_taxonomy.png",
       plot = tax_length_plot,
       width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 600)

```


```{r}
phage_by_country <- combined_data %>%
  select(sample_id, id, Country) %>%
  distinct() %>%
  group_by(Country, id) %>%
  summarise(count = n(), .groups = "drop")

phage_matrix <- phage_by_country %>%
  mutate(present = 1) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = Country, values_from = present, values_fill = 0)

library(UpSetR)

# Convert to data.frame for UpSetR (assuming phage_matrix is binary presence/absence)
phage_upset <- as.data.frame(phage_matrix)

# Improved UpSet plot
upset(
  phage_upset,
  sets = c("Uganda", "Kenya", "Tanzania"),
  order.by = "freq",
  keep.order = TRUE,
  sets.bar.color = "#1f78b4",           # Set bar color
  main.bar.color = "#33a02c",           # Intersection bar color
  matrix.color = "black",               # Dots in matrix
  text.scale = c(2, 2, 1.5, 1.5, 2, 2), # Customize font sizes
  mainbar.y.label = "Intersection Size",
  sets.x.label = "Samples per Country",
  number.angles = 0,
  point.size = 3,
  line.size = 1
)

```


```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

# Summarize unique phage IDs by country
phage_richness <- combined_data %>%
  select(sample_id, id, Country) %>%
  distinct() %>%
  group_by(Country) %>%
  summarise(unique_phages = n_distinct(id), .groups = "drop")

# Create the plot
ggplot(phage_richness, aes(x = reorder(Country, -unique_phages), y = unique_phages, fill = Country)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", width = 0.7, color = "black", show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_text(aes(label = unique_phages), vjust = -0.5, size = 5) +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Dark2") +
  labs(
    title = "Unique Prophage Richness Across Countries",
    x = "Country",
    y = "Number of Unique Prophage IDs"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 16, hjust = 0.5),
    axis.text = element_text(color = "black"),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold")
  )

```


```{r}
library(vegan)

# Create sample-level presence/absence matrix
phage_sample_matrix <- combined_data %>%
  select(sample_id, id) %>%
  distinct() %>%
  mutate(present = 1) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = id, values_from = present, values_fill = 0)

# Get metadata
sample_metadata <- combined_data %>%
  select(sample_id, Country) %>%
  distinct()

# PERMANOVA
adonis_result <- adonis2(
  phage_sample_matrix[,-1] ~ Country,
  data = sample_metadata,
  method = "jaccard"
)
print(adonis_result)

```


```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)

# Create binary matrix: rows = samples, columns = phage IDs
phage_sample_matrix <- combined_data %>%
  select(sample_id, id) %>%
  distinct() %>%
  mutate(present = 1) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = id, values_from = present, values_fill = 0)

```

```{r}
sample_metadata <- combined_data %>%
  select(sample_id, Country) %>%
  distinct()

```

```{r}
library(vegan)

# Remove sample_id column before ordination
phage_matrix <- phage_sample_matrix %>% column_to_rownames("sample_id")

# Run NMDS with Jaccard distance
set.seed(123)  # for reproducibility
nmds <- metaMDS(phage_matrix, distance = "jaccard", k = 2, trymax = 100)

# Extract NMDS scores
nmds_scores <- as.data.frame(nmds$points) %>%
  rownames_to_column("sample_id") %>%
  left_join(sample_metadata, by = "sample_id")

```


```{r}
library(ggplot2)

ggplot(nmds_scores, aes(x = MDS1, y = MDS2, color = Country)) +
  geom_point(size = 4, alpha = 0.85) +
  stat_ellipse(type = "t", linetype = "dashed", size = 1, alpha = 0.4) +
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "Set2") +
  labs(
    title = "NMDS Ordination of Phage Composition by Country",
    x = "NMDS1",
    y = "NMDS2",
    color = "Country"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 16, hjust = 0.5),
    axis.text = element_text(color = "black"),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    legend.position = "right"
  )

```

#Insertion sequences 
```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(stringr)

# Path to your main directory containing sample folders
main_dir <- "D:/mrsa/isescan_results"

# List all sample directories (assuming they are directly under main_dir)
sample_dirs <- list.dirs(path = main_dir, recursive = FALSE)

# Initialize an empty list to store dataframes
all_data <- list()

# Loop through each sample directory
for (dir_path in sample_dirs) {
  # Extract sample ID from directory name (e.g., 'ERR12511687')
  sample_id <- basename(dir_path)
  
  # Path to the TSV file
  tsv_path <- file.path(dir_path, "spades_output", "contigs.fasta.tsv")
  
  # Check if the file exists
  if (file.exists(tsv_path)) {
    # Read the TSV file
    df <- read.delim(tsv_path, sep = "\t", header = TRUE)
    
    # Add a column with sample ID
    df$sampleID <- sample_id
    
    # Store in list
    all_data[[sample_id]] <- df
  } else {
    message(paste("Missing TSV for sample:", sample_id))
  }
}

# Combine all dataframes into a single dataframe
merged_contigs <- bind_rows(all_data)

# Preview the merged data
head(merged_contigs)

```

```{r}
library(dplyr)

# Define sample IDs for each country
uganda_ids <- paste0("ERR", 4692235:4692276)

kenya_ids <- c(
  paste0("ERR", 3150908:3151001), "ERR3218227",
  paste0("ERR", 1764887:1765052),
  paste0("ERR", 1791869:1791882),
  paste0("ERR", 2436451:2436455)
)

tanzania_ids <- c(
  paste0("ERR", 12916291:12916300),
  paste0("ERR", 12511686:12511850)
)

# Add the 'Country' column based on sampleID
merged_contigs <- merged_contigs %>%
  mutate(
    Country = case_when(
      sampleID %in% uganda_ids ~ "Uganda",
      sampleID %in% kenya_ids ~ "Kenya",
      sampleID %in% tanzania_ids ~ "Tanzania",
      TRUE ~ "Unknown"
    )
  )

```

#Summarize IS Family Distribution
```{r}
library(dplyr)

# Assuming merged_contigs now has a 'Country' column
is_summary <- merged_contigs %>%
  group_by(Country, family) %>%
  summarise(
    count = n(),
    percentage = (n() / sum(n())) * 100
  ) %>%
  arrange(Country, desc(count))

```
```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(scales)  # For percent_format()

# Create the plot
p <- ggplot(is_summary, aes(x = Country, y = count, fill = family)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "fill") +
  labs(title = "Distribution of IS Families Across E.Africa",
       y = "Proportion",
       fill = "IS Family") +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = percent_format()) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    text = element_text(size=14),  # Increase font size for clarity
    legend.title = element_text(size=14),
    legend.text = element_text(size=12),
    plot.title = element_text(hjust=0.5, size=16, face="bold")
  )

# Save as high-resolution PNG
ggsave("is/IS_Family_Distribution.jpg", plot = p, width = 10, height = 8, dpi = 600)


```

```{r}
library(dplyr)

# Summarize counts
is_counts <- merged_contigs %>%
  group_by(Country, family) %>%
  summarise(count = n()) %>%
  ungroup()

```
```{r}
# Create contingency table
contingency_table <- table(merged_contigs$family, merged_contigs$Country)

# Perform chi-square test
chisq_result <- chisq.test(contingency_table)

# View results
print(chisq_result)

```

```{r}
library(dplyr)

# Loop over each family
for (fam in unique(merged_contigs$family)) {
  # Filter data for the specific family
  subset_data <- merged_contigs %>% filter(family == fam)
  
  # Create contingency table
  contingency <- table(subset_data$family, subset_data$Country)
  
  # Get table dimensions
  dims <- dim(contingency)
  
  # Check if table has at least 2 rows and 2 columns with data
  if (all(dims >= c(2, 2))) {
    # Perform chi-square test
    test_result <- chisq.test(contingency)
    cat("Family:", fam, "\n")
    print(test_result)
  } else if (all(dims == c(2, 2))) {
    # Perform Fisher's exact test for 2x2 table
    test_result <- fisher.test(contingency)
    cat("Family:", fam, " (2x2 table) - Fisher's Exact Test:\n")
    print(test_result)
  } else {
    # Insufficient variation for statistical test
    cat("Family:", fam, "has insufficient variation for chi-square or Fisher's test.\n")
  }
  cat("\n")  # Add space between outputs
}



```

```{r}
library(tidyr)
library(pheatmap)

# Prepare data matrix for heatmap
heatmap_data <- is_counts %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = family, values_from = count, values_fill = 0) %>%
  column_to_rownames(var = "Country") %>%
  as.matrix()

# Save as high-resolution JPEG
jpeg("is/IS_Family_Distribution_Heatmap.jpg", width = 12, height = 10, units = "in", res = 600)
pheatmap(heatmap_data, cluster_rows = TRUE, cluster_cols = TRUE,
         main = "IS Family Distribution Heatmap",
         fontsize = 12,
         color = colorRampPalette(c("blue", "white", "red"))(50))
dev.off()

```

```{r}
library(ggpubr)

# Calculate total IS per sample
sample_IS_counts <- merged_contigs %>%
  group_by(sampleID, Country) %>%
  summarise(total_IS = n())

# Save plot as high-resolution JPEG
jpeg("is/Total_IS_Elements_Per_Sample_by_Country.jpg", width = 12, height = 8, units = "in", res = 300)

# Generate the plot
ggplot(sample_IS_counts, aes(x=Country, y=total_IS, fill=Country)) +
  geom_violin() +
  geom_boxplot(width=0.2, alpha=0.5) +
  labs(title="Distribution of Total IS Elements per Sample by Country",
       y="Total IS Elements") +
  theme_minimal()

dev.off()


```

```{r}
# Load necessary libraries
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(igraph)
library(ggraph)

# Step 1: Prepare presence/absence matrix of IS families per sample
presence_data <- merged_contigs %>%
  distinct(sampleID, family) %>%
  mutate(present = 1) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = family, values_from = present, values_fill = list(present=0))

# Step 2: Compute co-occurrence matrix
family_matrix <- as.matrix(presence_data[,-1])  # remove sampleID
co_occurrence <- t(family_matrix) %*% family_matrix
diag(co_occurrence) <- 0  # Remove self-co-occurrence

# Step 3: Create edge list
edges <- as.data.frame(as.table(co_occurrence))
colnames(edges) <- c("family1", "family2", "weight")
edges <- edges %>%
  filter(weight > 0) %>%
  mutate(family_pair = ifelse(as.character(family1) < as.character(family2),
                              paste(family1, family2, sep = "_"),
                              paste(family2, family1, sep = "_"))) %>%
  distinct(family_pair, .keep_all = TRUE)

# Step 4: Create igraph object
g <- graph_from_data_frame(d=edges, directed=FALSE)

# Step 5: Save network plot as high-res JPEG
jpeg("is/IS_Cooccurrence_Network.jpg", width=12, height=10, units="in", res=300)

ggraph(g, layout='fr') + 
  geom_edge_link(aes(width=weight), alpha=0.8, color='gray') +
  geom_node_point(size=8, color='steelblue') +
  geom_node_text(aes(label=name), repel=TRUE, size=4) +
  theme_void() +
  labs(title="IS Co-occurrence Network") +
  theme(
    legend.position="none",
    plot.title=element_text(hjust=0.5, size=16, face="bold")
  )

dev.off()


```
#Phylogenetics
```{r}
    library(dplyr)

   # Load the data assuming it's saved as 'ani_output.csv'
   ani_data1 <- read.csv("D:/mrsa/dendogram/data_tables/Ndb.csv")  
   ani_data2 <- read.csv("D:/mrsa/dendogram/data_tables/Mdb.csv")
   ani_data3 <- read.csv("D:/mrsa/dendogram/data_tables/Cdb.csv")
   ani_data4 <- read.csv("D:/mrsa/dendogram/data_tables/Bdb.csv")
   
```


```{r}
# Load necessary library
library(dplyr)

# Define sample IDs for each country without the '.fasta' extension
uganda_ids <- paste0("ERR", 4692235:4692276)

kenya_ids <- c(
  paste0("ERR", 3150908:3151001), "ERR3218227",
  paste0("ERR", 1764887:1765052),
  paste0("ERR", 1791869:1791882),
  paste0("ERR", 2436451:2436455)
)

tanzania_ids <- c(
  paste0("ERR", 12916291:12916300),
  paste0("ERR", 12511686:12511850)
)

# Load your existing ani_data2
# Assuming it's already loaded as ani_data2
# ani_data2 <- read.csv("ani_output.csv", sep = "\t")

# Remove the '.fasta' from genome1 for matching
ani_data2 <- ani_data2 %>%
  mutate(genome1_id = sub("\\.fasta$", "", genome1))  # Create a new column without '.fasta'

# Add the 'Country' column based on sampleID in genome1_id
ani_data2 <- ani_data2 %>%
  mutate(
    Country = case_when(
      genome1_id %in% uganda_ids ~ "Uganda",
      genome1_id %in% kenya_ids ~ "Kenya",
      genome1_id %in% tanzania_ids ~ "Tanzania",
      TRUE ~ "Unknown"
    )
  )

# Check the updated ani_data2
print(ani_data2)

```

```{r}
# Change "Unknown" to "Refseq" in the Country column
ani_data2 <- ani_data2 %>%
  mutate(Country = ifelse(Country == "Unknown", "Refseq", Country))

# Check the updated ani_data2
print(head(ani_data2))

# Verify the summary of countries
country_summary <- table(ani_data2$Country)
print(country_summary)

```




```{r}
   # Create a wide format distance matrix
   distance_matrix <- as.data.frame(matrix(0, nrow = length(unique(ani_data2$genome1)), ncol = length(unique(ani_data2$genome2))))
   rownames(distance_matrix) <- unique(ani_data2$genome1)
   colnames(distance_matrix) <- unique(ani_data2$genome2)

   # Fill the distance matrix
   for (i in 1:nrow(ani_data2)) {
       distance_matrix[ani_data2$genome1[i], ani_data2$genome2[i]] <- ani_data2$dist[i]
       distance_matrix[ani_data2$genome2[i], ani_data2$genome1[i]] <- ani_data2$dist[i]  # Symmetric matrix
   }
   
```


```{r}
library(ape)
library(ggtree)
      # Assuming you have your distance matrix ready
   dist_matrix <- as.dist(distance_matrix)  # Create a distance object
   hc <- hclust(dist_matrix, method = "average")  # Hierarchical clustering
   phylo_tree <- as.phylo(hc)  # Convert to phylogenetic tree
   
```


```{r}
      # Plot the circular phylogenetic tree
   plot(phylo_tree, type = "fan", main = "Circular Phylogenetic Tree of Staphylococcus aureus")
   
   
```

```{r}
# Load required libraries
library(tidyverse)
library(ape)       # For tree construction
library(ggtree)    # For plotting

# Read your distance data
dist_df <- ani_data2 # Replace with actual filename

# Make a symmetrical matrix
dist_matrix <- dist_df %>%
  select(genome1, genome2, dist) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = genome2, values_from = dist) %>%
  column_to_rownames("genome1") %>%
  as.matrix()

# Ensure symmetry
dist_matrix[is.na(dist_matrix)] <- t(dist_matrix)[is.na(dist_matrix)]
dist_obj <- as.dist(dist_matrix)

```


```{r}
# Use UPGMA (hierarchical clustering)
tree_upgma <- hclust(dist_obj, method = "average")
plot(as.phylo(tree_upgma), main = "UPGMA Phylogenetic Tree")

# Or use neighbor-joining
tree_nj <- nj(dist_obj)
plot(tree_nj, main = "Neighbor-Joining Tree")

```

```{r}
# Use ggtree for publication-ready plots
library(ggtree)
p <- ggtree(tree_nj) + 
     geom_tiplab(size = 3) + 
     theme_tree2()
p

```

```{r}
# Load libraries
library(tidyverse)
library(ape)
library(stringr)  # Needed for string cleaning

# Step 1: Ensure genome names are consistent (remove .fasta suffix)
dist_df <- dist_df %>%
  mutate(genome1_id = str_remove(genome1_id, "\\.fasta$"),
         genome2 = str_remove(genome2, "\\.fasta$"))

# Step 2: Create lookup table for genome-country mapping
country_lookup <- dist_df %>%
  select(genome1_id, Country) %>%
  distinct()

# Step 3: Assign country information to genome2
dist_df <- dist_df %>%
  left_join(country_lookup, by = c("genome2" = "genome1_id")) %>%
  rename(Country1 = Country.x, Country2 = Country.y)

# Step 4: Function to filter distances and construct tree
plot_country_tree <- function(country_name) {
  message("Processing: ", country_name)

  # Filter only rows where both genomes are from the same country
  sub_df <- dist_df %>%
    filter(Country1 == country_name & Country2 == country_name)

  if (nrow(sub_df) == 0) {
    message("No data found for country: ", country_name)
    return(NULL)
  }

  # Get unique genome names
  genomes <- unique(c(sub_df$genome1, sub_df$genome2))

  # Create an empty distance matrix
  dist_mat <- matrix(0, nrow = length(genomes), ncol = length(genomes),
                     dimnames = list(genomes, genomes))

  # Fill the matrix with precomputed distances
  for (i in 1:nrow(sub_df)) {
    g1 <- sub_df$genome1[i]
    g2 <- sub_df$genome2[i]
    d <- sub_df$dist[i]
    dist_mat[g1, g2] <- d
    dist_mat[g2, g1] <- d
  }

  # Convert to 'dist' object
  dist_obj <- as.dist(dist_mat)

  # Build tree using Neighbor-Joining
  tree <- nj(dist_obj)

  # Plot
  plot(tree, main = paste("Phylogenetic Tree -", country_name), cex = 0.6)
  return(tree)
}

# Step 5: Generate trees for each country
tree_uganda <- plot_country_tree("Uganda")
tree_kenya <- plot_country_tree("Kenya")
tree_tanzania <- plot_country_tree("Tanzania")

```


```{r}
dist_df <- ani_data2 # Replace with actual filename

# Load required libraries
library(tidyverse)
library(ape)
library(stringr)

# Step 1: Ensure genome names are consistent (remove .fasta suffix)
dist_df <- dist_df %>%
  mutate(genome1 = str_remove(genome1, "\\.fasta$"),
         genome2 = str_remove(genome2, "\\.fasta$"),
         genome1_id = str_remove(genome1_id, "\\.fasta$"))

# Step 2: Create a country lookup from genome1 and its country
country_lookup <- dist_df %>%
  select(genome1_id, Country) %>%
  distinct()

# Step 3: Join to get Country2 based on genome2
dist_df <- dist_df %>%
  left_join(country_lookup, by = c("genome2" = "genome1_id")) %>%
  rename(Country1 = Country.x, Country2 = Country.y)

# Step 4: Function to plot circular phylogenetic tree for a country
plot_country_tree <- function(country_name) {
  message("Processing: ", country_name)
  
  # Filter data for the country
  sub_df <- dist_df %>%
    filter(Country1 == country_name & Country2 == country_name)
  
  if (nrow(sub_df) == 0) {
    message("No data found for country: ", country_name)
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  # Create distance matrix
  genomes <- unique(c(sub_df$genome1, sub_df$genome2))
  dist_mat <- matrix(NA, nrow = length(genomes), ncol = length(genomes),
                     dimnames = list(genomes, genomes))
  for (i in 1:nrow(sub_df)) {
    g1 <- sub_df$genome1[i]
    g2 <- sub_df$genome2[i]
    d <- sub_df$dist[i]
    dist_mat[g1, g2] <- d
    dist_mat[g2, g1] <- d
  }
  dist_mat[is.na(dist_mat)] <- max(sub_df$dist, na.rm=TRUE)*1.2
  diag(dist_mat) <- 0
  
  dist_obj <- as.dist(dist_mat)
  tree <- nj(dist_obj)
  
  # Create and customize plot
  ggtree_obj <- ggtree(tree, layout = "circular") +
    geom_tiplab(size=3, align=TRUE, offset=0.02) +
    theme_void() +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(size=16, face="bold"),
      plot.margin = margin(1, 1, 1, 1, "cm")
    ) +
    ggtitle(paste("Circular Phylogenetic Tree -", country_name))
  
  # Save high-resolution image
  ggsave(
    filename = paste0("phylo_tree_", tolower(country_name), ".png"),
    plot = ggtree_obj,
    width = 12,
    height = 12,
    dpi = 600,
    limitsize = FALSE
  )
  
  print(ggtree_obj)
  return(ggtree_obj)
}




```

```{r}
plot_country_tree <- function(country_name) {
  message("Processing: ", country_name)
  
  sub_df <- dist_df %>%
    filter(Country1 == country_name & Country2 == country_name)

  if (nrow(sub_df) == 0) {
    message("No data found for country: ", country_name)
    return(NULL)
  }

  genomes <- unique(c(sub_df$genome1, sub_df$genome2))
  
  dist_mat <- matrix(0, nrow = length(genomes), ncol = length(genomes),
                     dimnames = list(genomes, genomes))
  
  for (i in 1:nrow(sub_df)) {
    g1 <- sub_df$genome1[i]
    g2 <- sub_df$genome2[i]
    d <- sub_df$dist[i]
    dist_mat[g1, g2] <- d
    dist_mat[g2, g1] <- d
  }

  dist_obj <- as.dist(dist_mat)
  tree <- nj(dist_obj)

  # Circular tree with clearer labels
  plot(tree, type = "fan", main = paste("Circular Tree -", country_name),
       cex = 0.5,         # shrink label size
       label.offset = 0.05)  # push labels outward

  return(tree)
}

```
